1261     def _find_error_handler(self, e: Exception) -> t.Optional[ErrorHandlerCallable]:
1262         """Return a registered error handler for an exception in this order:
1263         blueprint handler for a specific code, app handler for a specific code,
1264         blueprint handler for an exception class, app handler for an exception
1265         class, or ``None`` if a suitable handler is not found.
1266         """
1267         exc_class, code = self._get_exc_class_and_code(type(e))
1268 
1269         for c in [code, None]:
1270             for name in chain(self._request_blueprints(), [None]):
1271                 handler_map = self.error_handler_spec[name][c]
1272 
1273                 if not handler_map:
1274                     continue
1275 
1276                 for cls in exc_class.__mro__:
1277                     handler = handler_map.get(cls)
1278 
1279                     if handler is not None:
1280                         return handler
1281         return None
1282 
1283     def handle_http_exception(
1284         self, e: HTTPException
1285     ) -> t.Union[HTTPException, ResponseReturnValue]:
1286         """Handles an HTTP exception.  By default this will invoke the
1287         registered error handlers and fall back to returning the
1288         exception as response.
1289 
1290         .. versionchanged:: 1.0.3
1291             ``RoutingException``, used internally for actions such as
1292              slash redirects during routing, is not passed to error
1293              handlers.
1294 
1295         .. versionchanged:: 1.0
1296             Exceptions are looked up by code *and* by MRO, so
1297             ``HTTPExcpetion`` subclasses can be handled with a catch-all
1298             handler for the base ``HTTPException``.
1299 
1300         .. versionadded:: 0.3
1301         """
1302         # Proxy exceptions don't have error codes.  We want to always return
1303         # those unchanged as errors
1304         if e.code is None:
1305             return e
1306 
1307         # RoutingExceptions are used internally to trigger routing
1308         # actions, such as slash redirects raising RequestRedirect. They
1309         # are not raised or handled in user code.
1310         if isinstance(e, RoutingException):
1311             return e
1312 
1313         handler = self._find_error_handler(e)
1314         if handler is None:
1315             return e
1316         return self.ensure_sync(handler)(e)
1317 
1318     def trap_http_exception(self, e: Exception) -> bool:
1319         """Checks if an HTTP exception should be trapped or not.  By default
1320         this will return ``False`` for all exceptions except for a bad request
1321         key error if ``TRAP_BAD_REQUEST_ERRORS`` is set to ``True``.  It
1322         also returns ``True`` if ``TRAP_HTTP_EXCEPTIONS`` is set to ``True``.
1323 
1324         This is called for all HTTP exceptions raised by a view function.
1325         If it returns ``True`` for any exception the error handler for this
1326         exception is not called and it shows up as regular exception in the
1327         traceback.  This is helpful for debugging implicitly raised HTTP
1328         exceptions.
1329 
1330         .. versionchanged:: 1.0
1331             Bad request errors are not trapped by default in debug mode.
1332 
1333         .. versionadded:: 0.8
1334         """
1335         if self.config["TRAP_HTTP_EXCEPTIONS"]:
1336             return True
1337 
1338         trap_bad_request = self.config["TRAP_BAD_REQUEST_ERRORS"]
1339 
1340         # if unset, trap key errors in debug mode
1341         if (
1342             trap_bad_request is None
1343             and self.debug
1344             and isinstance(e, BadRequestKeyError)
1345         ):
1346             return True
1347 
1348         if trap_bad_request:
1349             return isinstance(e, BadRequest)
1350 
1351         return False
1352 
1353     def handle_user_exception(
1354         self, e: Exception
1355     ) -> t.Union[HTTPException, ResponseReturnValue]:
1356         """This method is called whenever an exception occurs that
1357         should be handled. A special case is :class:`~werkzeug
1358         .exceptions.HTTPException` which is forwarded to the
1359         :meth:`handle_http_exception` method. This function will either
1360         return a response value or reraise the exception with the same
1361         traceback.
1362 
1363         .. versionchanged:: 1.0
1364             Key errors raised from request data like ``form`` show the
1365             bad key in debug mode rather than a generic bad request
1366             message.
1367 
1368         .. versionadded:: 0.7
1369         """
1370         if isinstance(e, BadRequestKeyError) and (
1371             self.debug or self.config["TRAP_BAD_REQUEST_ERRORS"]
1372         ):
1373             e.show_exception = True
1374 
1375         if isinstance(e, HTTPException) and not self.trap_http_exception(e):
1376             return self.handle_http_exception(e)
1377 
1378         handler = self._find_error_handler(e)
1379 
1380         if handler is None:
1381             raise
1382 
1383         return self.ensure_sync(handler)(e)
1384 
1385     def handle_exception(self, e: Exception) -> Response:
1386         """Handle an exception that did not have an error handler
1387         associated with it, or that was raised from an error handler.
1388         This always causes a 500 ``InternalServerError``.
1389 
1390         Always sends the :data:`got_request_exception` signal.
1391 
1392         If :attr:`propagate_exceptions` is ``True``, such as in debug
1393         mode, the error will be re-raised so that the debugger can
1394         display it. Otherwise, the original exception is logged, and
1395         an :exc:`~werkzeug.exceptions.InternalServerError` is returned.
1396 
1397         If an error handler is registered for ``InternalServerError`` or
1398         ``500``, it will be used. For consistency, the handler will
1399         always receive the ``InternalServerError``. The original
1400         unhandled exception is available as ``e.original_exception``.
1401 
1402         .. versionchanged:: 1.1.0
1403             Always passes the ``InternalServerError`` instance to the
1404             handler, setting ``original_exception`` to the unhandled
1405             error.
1406 
1407         .. versionchanged:: 1.1.0
1408             ``after_request`` functions and other finalization is done
1409             even for the default 500 response when there is no handler.
1410 
1411         .. versionadded:: 0.3
1412         """
1413         exc_info = sys.exc_info()
1414         got_request_exception.send(self, exception=e)
1415 
1416         if self.propagate_exceptions:
1417             # Re-raise if called with an active exception, otherwise
1418             # raise the passed in exception.
1419             if exc_info[1] is e:
1420                 raise
1421 
1422             raise e
1423 
1424         self.log_exception(exc_info)
1425         server_error: t.Union[InternalServerError, ResponseReturnValue]
1426         server_error = InternalServerError(original_exception=e)
1427         handler = self._find_error_handler(server_error)
1428 
1429         if handler is not None:
1430             server_error = self.ensure_sync(handler)(server_error)
1431 
1432         return self.finalize_request(server_error, from_error_handler=True)
1433 
1434     def log_exception(
1435         self,
1436         exc_info: t.Union[
1437             t.Tuple[type, BaseException, TracebackType], t.Tuple[None, None, None]
1438         ],
1439     ) -> None:
1440         """Logs an exception.  This is called by :meth:`handle_exception`
1441         if debugging is disabled and right before the handler is called.
1442         The default implementation logs the exception as error on the
1443         :attr:`logger`.
1444 
1445         .. versionadded:: 0.8
1446         """
1447         self.logger.error(
1448             f"Exception on {request.path} [{request.method}]", exc_info=exc_info
1449         )
1450 
1451     def raise_routing_exception(self, request: Request) -> "te.NoReturn":
1452         """Exceptions that are recording during routing are reraised with
1453         this method.  During debug we are not reraising redirect requests
1454         for non ``GET``, ``HEAD``, or ``OPTIONS`` requests and we're raising
1455         a different error instead to help debug situations.
1456 
1457         :internal:
1458         """
1459         if (
1460             not self.debug
1461             or not isinstance(request.routing_exception, RequestRedirect)
1462             or request.method in ("GET", "HEAD", "OPTIONS")
1463         ):
1464             raise request.routing_exception  # type: ignore
1465 
1466         from .debughelpers import FormDataRoutingRedirect
1467 
1468         raise FormDataRoutingRedirect(request)
1469 
1470     def dispatch_request(self) -> ResponseReturnValue:
1471         """Does the request dispatching.  Matches the URL and returns the
1472         return value of the view or error handler.  This does not have to
1473         be a response object.  In order to convert the return value to a
1474         proper response object, call :func:`make_response`.
1475 
1476         .. versionchanged:: 0.7
1477            This no longer does the exception handling, this code was
1478            moved to the new :meth:`full_dispatch_request`.
1479         """
1480         req = _request_ctx_stack.top.request
1481         if req.routing_exception is not None:
1482             self.raise_routing_exception(req)
1483         rule = req.url_rule
1484         # if we provide automatic options for this URL and the
1485         # request came with the OPTIONS method, reply automatically
1486         if (
1487             getattr(rule, "provide_automatic_options", False)
1488             and req.method == "OPTIONS"
1489         ):
1490             return self.make_default_options_response()
1491         # otherwise dispatch to the handler for that endpoint
1492         return self.ensure_sync(self.view_functions[rule.endpoint])(**req.view_args)
1493 
1494     def full_dispatch_request(self) -> Response:
1495         """Dispatches the request and on top of that performs request
1496         pre and postprocessing as well as HTTP exception catching and
1497         error handling.
1498 
1499         .. versionadded:: 0.7
1500         """
1501         self.try_trigger_before_first_request_functions()
1502         try:
1503             request_started.send(self)
1504             rv = self.preprocess_request()
1505             if rv is None:
1506                 rv = self.dispatch_request()
1507         except Exception as e:
1508             rv = self.handle_user_exception(e)
1509         return self.finalize_request(rv)
1510 
1511     def finalize_request(
1512         self,
1513         rv: t.Union[ResponseReturnValue, HTTPException],
1514         from_error_handler: bool = False,
1515     ) -> Response:
1516         """Given the return value from a view function this finalizes
1517         the request by converting it into a response and invoking the
1518         postprocessing functions.  This is invoked for both normal
1519         request dispatching as well as error handlers.
1520 
1521         Because this means that it might be called as a result of a
1522         failure a special safe mode is available which can be enabled
1523         with the `from_error_handler` flag.  If enabled, failures in
1524         response processing will be logged and otherwise ignored.
1525 
1526         :internal:
1527         """
1528         response = self.make_response(rv)
1529         try:
1530             response = self.process_response(response)
1531             request_finished.send(self, response=response)
1532         except Exception:
1533             if not from_error_handler:
1534                 raise
1535             self.logger.exception(
1536                 "Request finalizing failed with an error while handling an error"
1537             )
1538         return response
1539 
1540     def try_trigger_before_first_request_functions(self) -> None:
1541         """Called before each request and will ensure that it triggers
1542         the :attr:`before_first_request_funcs` and only exactly once per
1543         application instance (which means process usually).
1544 
1545         :internal:
1546         """
1547         if self._got_first_request:
1548             return
1549         with self._before_request_lock:
1550             if self._got_first_request:
1551                 return
1552             for func in self.before_first_request_funcs:
1553                 self.ensure_sync(func)()
1554             self._got_first_request = True
1555 
1556     def make_default_options_response(self) -> Response:
1557         """This method is called to create the default ``OPTIONS`` response.
1558         This can be changed through subclassing to change the default
1559         behavior of ``OPTIONS`` responses.
1560 
1561         .. versionadded:: 0.7
1562         """
1563         adapter = _request_ctx_stack.top.url_adapter
1564         methods = adapter.allowed_methods()
1565         rv = self.response_class()
1566         rv.allow.update(methods)
1567         return rv
1568 
1569     def should_ignore_error(self, error: t.Optional[BaseException]) -> bool:
1570         """This is called to figure out if an error should be ignored
1571         or not as far as the teardown system is concerned.  If this
1572         function returns ``True`` then the teardown handlers will not be
1573         passed the error.
1574 
1575         .. versionadded:: 0.10
1576         """
1577         return False
1578 
1579     def ensure_sync(self, func: t.Callable) -> t.Callable:
1580         """Ensure that the function is synchronous for WSGI workers.
1581         Plain ``def`` functions are returned as-is. ``async def``
1582         functions are wrapped to run and wait for the response.
1583 
1584         Override this method to change how the app runs async views.
1585 
1586         .. versionadded:: 2.0
1587         """
1588         if iscoroutinefunction(func):
1589             return self.async_to_sync(func)
1590 
1591         return func
1592 
1593     def async_to_sync(
1594         self, func: t.Callable[..., t.Coroutine]
1595     ) -> t.Callable[..., t.Any]:
1596         """Return a sync function that will run the coroutine function.
1597 
1598         .. code-block:: python
1599 
1600             result = app.async_to_sync(func)(*args, **kwargs)
1601 
1602         Override this method to change how the app converts async code
1603         to be synchronously callable.
1604 
1605         .. versionadded:: 2.0
1606         """
1607         try:
1608             from asgiref.sync import async_to_sync as asgiref_async_to_sync
1609         except ImportError:
1610             raise RuntimeError(
1611                 "Install Flask with the 'async' extra in order to use async views."
1612             )
1613 
1614         # Check that Werkzeug isn't using its fallback ContextVar class.
1615         if ContextVar.__module__ == "werkzeug.local":
1616             raise RuntimeError(
1617                 "Async cannot be used with this combination of Python "
1618                 "and Greenlet versions."
1619             )
1620 
1621         return asgiref_async_to_sync(func)
1622 
1623     def make_response(self, rv: ResponseReturnValue) -> Response:
1624         """Convert the return value from a view function to an instance of
1625         :attr:`response_class`.
1626 
1627         :param rv: the return value from the view function. The view function
1628             must return a response. Returning ``None``, or the view ending
1629             without returning, is not allowed. The following types are allowed
1630             for ``view_rv``:
1631 
1632             ``str``
1633                 A response object is created with the string encoded to UTF-8
1634                 as the body.
1635 
1636             ``bytes``
1637                 A response object is created with the bytes as the body.
1638 
1639             ``dict``
1640                 A dictionary that will be jsonify'd before being returned.
1641 
1642             ``tuple``
1643                 Either ``(body, status, headers)``, ``(body, status)``, or
1644                 ``(body, headers)``, where ``body`` is any of the other types
1645                 allowed here, ``status`` is a string or an integer, and
1646                 ``headers`` is a dictionary or a list of ``(key, value)``
1647                 tuples. If ``body`` is a :attr:`response_class` instance,
1648                 ``status`` overwrites the exiting value and ``headers`` are
1649                 extended.
1650 
1651             :attr:`response_class`
1652                 The object is returned unchanged.
1653 
1654             other :class:`~werkzeug.wrappers.Response` class
1655                 The object is coerced to :attr:`response_class`.
1656 
1657             :func:`callable`
1658                 The function is called as a WSGI application. The result is
1659                 used to create a response object.
1660 
1661         .. versionchanged:: 0.9
1662            Previously a tuple was interpreted as the arguments for the
1663            response object.
1664         """
1665 
1666         status = headers = None
1667 
1668         # unpack tuple returns
1669         if isinstance(rv, tuple):
1670             len_rv = len(rv)
1671 
1672             # a 3-tuple is unpacked directly
1673             if len_rv == 3:
1674                 rv, status, headers = rv
1675             # decide if a 2-tuple has status or headers
1676             elif len_rv == 2:
1677                 if isinstance(rv[1], (Headers, dict, tuple, list)):
1678                     rv, headers = rv
1679                 else:
1680                     rv, status = rv
1681             # other sized tuples are not allowed
1682             else:
1683                 raise TypeError(
1684                     "The view function did not return a valid response tuple."
1685                     " The tuple must have the form (body, status, headers),"
1686                     " (body, status), or (body, headers)."
1687                 )
1688 
1689         # the body must not be None
1690         if rv is None:
1691             raise TypeError(
1692                 f"The view function for {request.endpoint!r} did not"
1693                 " return a valid response. The function either returned"
1694                 " None or ended without a return statement."
1695             )
1696 
1697         # make sure the body is an instance of the response class
1698         if not isinstance(rv, self.response_class):
1699             if isinstance(rv, (str, bytes, bytearray)):
1700                 # let the response class set the status and headers instead of
1701                 # waiting to do it manually, so that the class can handle any
1702                 # special logic
1703                 rv = self.response_class(rv, status=status, headers=headers)
1704                 status = headers = None
1705             elif isinstance(rv, dict):
1706                 rv = jsonify(rv)
1707             elif isinstance(rv, BaseResponse) or callable(rv):
1708                 # evaluate a WSGI callable, or coerce a different response
1709                 # class to the correct type
1710                 try:
1711                     rv = self.response_class.force_type(rv, request.environ)  # type: ignore  # noqa: B950
1712                 except TypeError as e:
1713                     raise TypeError(
1714                         f"{e}\nThe view function did not return a valid"
1715                         " response. The return type must be a string,"
1716                         " dict, tuple, Response instance, or WSGI"
1717                         f" callable, but it was a {type(rv).__name__}."
1718                     ).with_traceback(sys.exc_info()[2])
1719             else:
1720                 raise TypeError(
1721                     "The view function did not return a valid"
1722                     " response. The return type must be a string,"
1723                     " dict, tuple, Response instance, or WSGI"
1724                     f" callable, but it was a {type(rv).__name__}."
1725                 )
1726 
1727         rv = t.cast(Response, rv)
1728         # prefer the status if it was provided
1729         if status is not None:
1730             if isinstance(status, (str, bytes, bytearray)):
1731                 rv.status = status  # type: ignore
1732             else:
1733                 rv.status_code = status
1734 
1735         # extend existing headers with provided headers
1736         if headers:
1737             rv.headers.update(headers)
1738 
1739         return rv
1740 
1741     def create_url_adapter(
1742         self, request: t.Optional[Request]
1743     ) -> t.Optional[MapAdapter]:
1744         """Creates a URL adapter for the given request. The URL adapter
1745         is created at a point where the request context is not yet set
1746         up so the request is passed explicitly.
1747 
1748         .. versionadded:: 0.6
1749 
1750         .. versionchanged:: 0.9
1751            This can now also be called without a request object when the
1752            URL adapter is created for the application context.
1753 
1754         .. versionchanged:: 1.0
1755             :data:`SERVER_NAME` no longer implicitly enables subdomain
1756             matching. Use :attr:`subdomain_matching` instead.
1757         """
1758         if request is not None:
1759             # If subdomain matching is disabled (the default), use the
1760             # default subdomain in all cases. This should be the default
1761             # in Werkzeug but it currently does not have that feature.
1762             if not self.subdomain_matching:
1763                 subdomain = self.url_map.default_subdomain or None
1764             else:
1765                 subdomain = None
1766 
1767             return self.url_map.bind_to_environ(
1768                 request.environ,
1769                 server_name=self.config["SERVER_NAME"],
1770                 subdomain=subdomain,
1771             )
1772         # We need at the very least the server name to be set for this
1773         # to work.
1774         if self.config["SERVER_NAME"] is not None:
1775             return self.url_map.bind(
1776                 self.config["SERVER_NAME"],
1777                 script_name=self.config["APPLICATION_ROOT"],
1778                 url_scheme=self.config["PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME"],
1779             )
1780 
1781         return None
1782 
1783     def inject_url_defaults(self, endpoint: str, values: dict) -> None:
1784         """Injects the URL defaults for the given endpoint directly into
1785         the values dictionary passed.  This is used internally and
1786         automatically called on URL building.
1787 
1788         .. versionadded:: 0.7
1789         """
1790         funcs: t.Iterable[URLDefaultCallable] = self.url_default_functions[None]
1791         if "." in endpoint:
1792             bp = endpoint.rsplit(".", 1)[0]
1793             funcs = chain(funcs, self.url_default_functions[bp])
1794         for func in funcs:
1795             func(endpoint, values)
1796 
1797     def handle_url_build_error(
1798         self, error: Exception, endpoint: str, values: dict
1799     ) -> str:
1800         """Handle :class:`~werkzeug.routing.BuildError` on
1801         :meth:`url_for`.
1802         """
1803         for handler in self.url_build_error_handlers:
1804             try:
1805                 rv = handler(error, endpoint, values)
1806             except BuildError as e:
1807                 # make error available outside except block
1808                 error = e
1809             else:
1810                 if rv is not None:
1811                     return rv
1812 
1813         # Re-raise if called with an active exception, otherwise raise
1814         # the passed in exception.
1815         if error is sys.exc_info()[1]:
1816             raise
1817 
1818         raise error
1819 
1820     def preprocess_request(self) -> t.Optional[ResponseReturnValue]:
1821         """Called before the request is dispatched. Calls
1822         :attr:`url_value_preprocessors` registered with the app and the
1823         current blueprint (if any). Then calls :attr:`before_request_funcs`
1824         registered with the app and the blueprint.
1825 
1826         If any :meth:`before_request` handler returns a non-None value, the
1827         value is handled as if it was the return value from the view, and
1828         further request handling is stopped.
1829         """
1830 
1831         funcs: t.Iterable[URLValuePreprocessorCallable] = self.url_value_preprocessors[
1832             None
1833         ]
1834         for bp in self._request_blueprints():
1835             if bp in self.url_value_preprocessors:
1836                 funcs = chain(funcs, self.url_value_preprocessors[bp])
1837         for func in funcs:
1838             func(request.endpoint, request.view_args)
1839 
1840         funcs: t.Iterable[BeforeRequestCallable] = self.before_request_funcs[None]
1841         for bp in self._request_blueprints():
1842             if bp in self.before_request_funcs:
1843                 funcs = chain(funcs, self.before_request_funcs[bp])
1844         for func in funcs:
1845             rv = self.ensure_sync(func)()
1846             if rv is not None:
1847                 return rv
1848 
1849         return None
1850 
1851     def process_response(self, response: Response) -> Response:
1852         """Can be overridden in order to modify the response object
1853         before it's sent to the WSGI server.  By default this will
1854         call all the :meth:`after_request` decorated functions.
1855 
1856         .. versionchanged:: 0.5
1857            As of Flask 0.5 the functions registered for after request
1858            execution are called in reverse order of registration.
1859 
1860         :param response: a :attr:`response_class` object.
1861         :return: a new response object or the same, has to be an
1862                  instance of :attr:`response_class`.
1863         """
1864         ctx = _request_ctx_stack.top
1865         funcs: t.Iterable[AfterRequestCallable] = ctx._after_request_functions
1866         for bp in self._request_blueprints():
1867             if bp in self.after_request_funcs:
1868                 funcs = chain(funcs, reversed(self.after_request_funcs[bp]))
1869         if None in self.after_request_funcs:
1870             funcs = chain(funcs, reversed(self.after_request_funcs[None]))
1871         for handler in funcs:
1872             response = self.ensure_sync(handler)(response)
1873         if not self.session_interface.is_null_session(ctx.session):
1874             self.session_interface.save_session(self, ctx.session, response)
1875         return response
1876 
1877     def do_teardown_request(
1878         self, exc: t.Optional[BaseException] = _sentinel  # type: ignore
1879     ) -> None:
1880         """Called after the request is dispatched and the response is
1881         returned, right before the request context is popped.
1882 
1883         This calls all functions decorated with
1884         :meth:`teardown_request`, and :meth:`Blueprint.teardown_request`
1885         if a blueprint handled the request. Finally, the
1886         :data:`request_tearing_down` signal is sent.
1887 
1888         This is called by
1889         :meth:`RequestContext.pop() <flask.ctx.RequestContext.pop>`,
1890         which may be delayed during testing to maintain access to
1891         resources.
1892 
1893         :param exc: An unhandled exception raised while dispatching the
1894             request. Detected from the current exception information if
1895             not passed. Passed to each teardown function.
1896 
1897         .. versionchanged:: 0.9
1898             Added the ``exc`` argument.
1899         """
1900         if exc is _sentinel:
1901             exc = sys.exc_info()[1]
1902         funcs: t.Iterable[TeardownCallable] = reversed(
1903             self.teardown_request_funcs[None]
1904         )
1905         for bp in self._request_blueprints():
1906             if bp in self.teardown_request_funcs:
1907                 funcs = chain(funcs, reversed(self.teardown_request_funcs[bp]))
1908         for func in funcs:
1909             self.ensure_sync(func)(exc)
1910         request_tearing_down.send(self, exc=exc)
1911 
1912     def do_teardown_appcontext(
1913         self, exc: t.Optional[BaseException] = _sentinel  # type: ignore
1914     ) -> None:
1915         """Called right before the application context is popped.
1916 
1917         When handling a request, the application context is popped
1918         after the request context. See :meth:`do_teardown_request`.
1919 
1920         This calls all functions decorated with
1921         :meth:`teardown_appcontext`. Then the
1922         :data:`appcontext_tearing_down` signal is sent.
1923 
1924         This is called by
1925         :meth:`AppContext.pop() <flask.ctx.AppContext.pop>`.
1926 
1927         .. versionadded:: 0.9
1928         """
1929         if exc is _sentinel:
1930             exc = sys.exc_info()[1]
1931         for func in reversed(self.teardown_appcontext_funcs):
1932             self.ensure_sync(func)(exc)
1933         appcontext_tearing_down.send(self, exc=exc)
1934 
1935     def app_context(self) -> AppContext:
1936         """Create an :class:`~flask.ctx.AppContext`. Use as a ``with``
1937         block to push the context, which will make :data:`current_app`
1938         point at this application.
1939 
1940         An application context is automatically pushed by
1941         :meth:`RequestContext.push() <flask.ctx.RequestContext.push>`
1942         when handling a request, and when running a CLI command. Use
1943         this to manually create a context outside of these situations.
1944 
1945         ::
1946 
1947             with app.app_context():
1948                 init_db()
1949 
1950         See :doc:`/appcontext`.
1951 
1952         .. versionadded:: 0.9
1953         """
1954         return AppContext(self)
1955 
1956     def request_context(self, environ: dict) -> RequestContext:
1957         """Create a :class:`~flask.ctx.RequestContext` representing a
1958         WSGI environment. Use a ``with`` block to push the context,
1959         which will make :data:`request` point at this request.
1960 
1961         See :doc:`/reqcontext`.
1962 
1963         Typically you should not call this from your own code. A request
1964         context is automatically pushed by the :meth:`wsgi_app` when
1965         handling a request. Use :meth:`test_request_context` to create
1966         an environment and context instead of this method.
1967 
1968         :param environ: a WSGI environment
1969         """
1970         return RequestContext(self, environ)
1971 
1972     def test_request_context(self, *args: t.Any, **kwargs: t.Any) -> RequestContext:
1973         """Create a :class:`~flask.ctx.RequestContext` for a WSGI
1974         environment created from the given values. This is mostly useful
1975         during testing, where you may want to run a function that uses
1976         request data without dispatching a full request.
1977 
1978         See :doc:`/reqcontext`.
1979 
1980         Use a ``with`` block to push the context, which will make
1981         :data:`request` point at the request for the created
1982         environment. ::
1983 
1984             with test_request_context(...):
1985                 generate_report()
1986 
1987         When using the shell, it may be easier to push and pop the
1988         context manually to avoid indentation. ::
1989 
1990             ctx = app.test_request_context(...)
1991             ctx.push()
1992             ...
1993             ctx.pop()
1994 
1995         Takes the same arguments as Werkzeug's
1996         :class:`~werkzeug.test.EnvironBuilder`, with some defaults from
1997         the application. See the linked Werkzeug docs for most of the
1998         available arguments. Flask-specific behavior is listed here.
1999 
2000         :param path: URL path being requested.
2001         :param base_url: Base URL where the app is being served, which
2002             ``path`` is relative to. If not given, built from
2003             :data:`PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME`, ``subdomain``,
2004             :data:`SERVER_NAME`, and :data:`APPLICATION_ROOT`.
2005         :param subdomain: Subdomain name to append to
2006             :data:`SERVER_NAME`.
2007         :param url_scheme: Scheme to use instead of
2008             :data:`PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME`.
2009         :param data: The request body, either as a string or a dict of
2010             form keys and values.
2011         :param json: If given, this is serialized as JSON and passed as
2012             ``data``. Also defaults ``content_type`` to
2013             ``application/json``.
2014         :param args: other positional arguments passed to
2015             :class:`~werkzeug.test.EnvironBuilder`.
2016         :param kwargs: other keyword arguments passed to
2017             :class:`~werkzeug.test.EnvironBuilder`.
2018         """
2019         from .testing import EnvironBuilder
2020 
2021         builder = EnvironBuilder(self, *args, **kwargs)
2022 
2023         try:
2024             return self.request_context(builder.get_environ())
2025         finally:
2026             builder.close()
2027 
2028     def wsgi_app(self, environ: dict, start_response: t.Callable) -> t.Any:
2029         """The actual WSGI application. This is not implemented in
2030         :meth:`__call__` so that middlewares can be applied without
2031         losing a reference to the app object. Instead of doing this::
2032 
2033             app = MyMiddleware(app)
2034 
2035         It's a better idea to do this instead::
2036 
2037             app.wsgi_app = MyMiddleware(app.wsgi_app)
2038 
2039         Then you still have the original application object around and
2040         can continue to call methods on it.
2041 
2042         .. versionchanged:: 0.7
2043             Teardown events for the request and app contexts are called
2044             even if an unhandled error occurs. Other events may not be
2045             called depending on when an error occurs during dispatch.
2046             See :ref:`callbacks-and-errors`.
2047 
2048         :param environ: A WSGI environment.
2049         :param start_response: A callable accepting a status code,
2050             a list of headers, and an optional exception context to
2051             start the response.
2052         """
2053         ctx = self.request_context(environ)
2054         error: t.Optional[BaseException] = None
2055         try:
2056             try:
2057                 ctx.push()
2058                 response = self.full_dispatch_request()
2059             except Exception as e:
2060                 error = e
2061                 response = self.handle_exception(e)
2062             except:  # noqa: B001
2063                 error = sys.exc_info()[1]
2064                 raise
2065             return response(environ, start_response)
2066         finally:
2067             if self.should_ignore_error(error):
2068                 error = None
2069             ctx.auto_pop(error)
2070 
2071     def __call__(self, environ: dict, start_response: t.Callable) -> t.Any:
2072         """The WSGI server calls the Flask application object as the
2073         WSGI application. This calls :meth:`wsgi_app`, which can be
2074         wrapped to apply middleware.
2075         """
2076         return self.wsgi_app(environ, start_response)
2077 
2078     def _request_blueprints(self) -> t.Iterable[str]:
2079         if _request_ctx_stack.top.request.blueprint is None:
2080             return []
2081         else:
2082             return reversed(_request_ctx_stack.top.request.blueprint.split("."))

25 class BlueprintSetupState:
26     """Temporary holder object for registering a blueprint with the
27     application.  An instance of this class is created by the
28     :meth:`~flask.Blueprint.make_setup_state` method and later passed
29     to all register callback functions.
30     """
31 
32     def __init__(
33         self,
34         blueprint: "Blueprint",
35         app: "Flask",
36         options: t.Any,
37         first_registration: bool,
38     ) -> None:
39         #: a reference to the current application
40         self.app = app
41 
42         #: a reference to the blueprint that created this setup state.
43         self.blueprint = blueprint
44 
45         #: a dictionary with all options that were passed to the
46         #: :meth:`~flask.Flask.register_blueprint` method.
47         self.options = options
48 
49         #: as blueprints can be registered multiple times with the
50         #: application and not everything wants to be registered
51         #: multiple times on it, this attribute can be used to figure
52         #: out if the blueprint was registered in the past already.
53         self.first_registration = first_registration
54 
55         subdomain = self.options.get("subdomain")
56         if subdomain is None:
57             subdomain = self.blueprint.subdomain
58 
59         #: The subdomain that the blueprint should be active for, ``None``
60         #: otherwise.
61         self.subdomain = subdomain
62 
63         url_prefix = self.options.get("url_prefix")
64         if url_prefix is None:
65             url_prefix = self.blueprint.url_prefix
66         #: The prefix that should be used for all URLs defined on the
67         #: blueprint.
68         self.url_prefix = url_prefix
69 
70         self.name_prefix = self.options.get("name_prefix", "")
71 
72         #: A dictionary with URL defaults that is added to each and every
73         #: URL that was defined with the blueprint.
74         self.url_defaults = dict(self.blueprint.url_values_defaults)
75         self.url_defaults.update(self.options.get("url_defaults", ()))
76 
77     def add_url_rule(
78         self,
79         rule: str,
80         endpoint: t.Optional[str] = None,
81         view_func: t.Optional[t.Callable] = None,
82         **options: t.Any,
83     ) -> None:
84         """A helper method to register a rule (and optionally a view function)
85         to the application.  The endpoint is automatically prefixed with the
86         blueprint's name.
87         """
88         if self.url_prefix is not None:
89             if rule:
90                 rule = "/".join((self.url_prefix.rstrip("/"), rule.lstrip("/")))
91             else:
92                 rule = self.url_prefix
93         options.setdefault("subdomain", self.subdomain)
94         if endpoint is None:
95             endpoint = _endpoint_from_view_func(view_func)  # type: ignore
96         defaults = self.url_defaults
97         if "defaults" in options:
98             defaults = dict(defaults, **options.pop("defaults"))
99         self.app.add_url_rule(
100             rule,
101             f"{self.name_prefix}{self.blueprint.name}.{endpoint}",
102             view_func,
103             defaults=defaults,
104             **options,
105         )

108 class Blueprint(Scaffold):
109     """Represents a blueprint, a collection of routes and other
110     app-related functions that can be registered on a real application
111     later.
112 
113     A blueprint is an object that allows defining application functions
114     without requiring an application object ahead of time. It uses the
115     same decorators as :class:`~flask.Flask`, but defers the need for an
116     application by recording them for later registration.
117 
118     Decorating a function with a blueprint creates a deferred function
119     that is called with :class:`~flask.blueprints.BlueprintSetupState`
120     when the blueprint is registered on an application.
121 
122     See :doc:`/blueprints` for more information.
123 
124     :param name: The name of the blueprint. Will be prepended to each
125         endpoint name.
126     :param import_name: The name of the blueprint package, usually
127         ``__name__``. This helps locate the ``root_path`` for the
128         blueprint.
129     :param static_folder: A folder with static files that should be
130         served by the blueprint's static route. The path is relative to
131         the blueprint's root path. Blueprint static files are disabled
132         by default.
133     :param static_url_path: The url to serve static files from.
134         Defaults to ``static_folder``. If the blueprint does not have
135         a ``url_prefix``, the app's static route will take precedence,
136         and the blueprint's static files won't be accessible.
137     :param template_folder: A folder with templates that should be added
138         to the app's template search path. The path is relative to the
139         blueprint's root path. Blueprint templates are disabled by
140         default. Blueprint templates have a lower precedence than those
141         in the app's templates folder.
142     :param url_prefix: A path to prepend to all of the blueprint's URLs,
143         to make them distinct from the rest of the app's routes.
144     :param subdomain: A subdomain that blueprint routes will match on by
145         default.
146     :param url_defaults: A dict of default values that blueprint routes
147         will receive by default.
148     :param root_path: By default, the blueprint will automatically set
149         this based on ``import_name``. In certain situations this
150         automatic detection can fail, so the path can be specified
151         manually instead.
152 
153     .. versionchanged:: 1.1.0
154         Blueprints have a ``cli`` group to register nested CLI commands.
155         The ``cli_group`` parameter controls the name of the group under
156         the ``flask`` command.
157 
158     .. versionadded:: 0.7
159     """
160 
161     warn_on_modifications = False
162     _got_registered_once = False
163 
164     #: Blueprint local JSON encoder class to use. Set to ``None`` to use
165     #: the app's :class:`~flask.Flask.json_encoder`.
166     json_encoder = None
167     #: Blueprint local JSON decoder class to use. Set to ``None`` to use
168     #: the app's :class:`~flask.Flask.json_decoder`.
169     json_decoder = None
170 
171     def __init__(
172         self,
173         name: str,
174         import_name: str,
175         static_folder: t.Optional[str] = None,
176         static_url_path: t.Optional[str] = None,
177         template_folder: t.Optional[str] = None,
178         url_prefix: t.Optional[str] = None,
179         subdomain: t.Optional[str] = None,
180         url_defaults: t.Optional[dict] = None,
181         root_path: t.Optional[str] = None,
182         cli_group: t.Optional[str] = _sentinel,  # type: ignore
183     ):
184         super().__init__(
185             import_name=import_name,
186             static_folder=static_folder,
187             static_url_path=static_url_path,
188             template_folder=template_folder,
189             root_path=root_path,
190         )
191 
192         if "." in name:
193             raise ValueError("'name' may not contain a dot '.' character.")
194 
195         self.name = name
196         self.url_prefix = url_prefix
197         self.subdomain = subdomain
198         self.deferred_functions: t.List[DeferredSetupFunction] = []
199 
200         if url_defaults is None:
201             url_defaults = {}
202 
203         self.url_values_defaults = url_defaults
204         self.cli_group = cli_group
205         self._blueprints: t.List[t.Tuple["Blueprint", dict]] = []
206 
207     def _is_setup_finished(self) -> bool:
208         return self.warn_on_modifications and self._got_registered_once
209 
210     def record(self, func: t.Callable) -> None:
211         """Registers a function that is called when the blueprint is
212         registered on the application.  This function is called with the
213         state as argument as returned by the :meth:`make_setup_state`
214         method.
215         """
216         if self._got_registered_once and self.warn_on_modifications:
217             from warnings import warn
218 
219             warn(
220                 Warning(
221                     "The blueprint was already registered once but is"
222                     " getting modified now. These changes will not show"
223                     " up."
224                 )
225             )
226         self.deferred_functions.append(func)
227 
228     def record_once(self, func: t.Callable) -> None:
229         """Works like :meth:`record` but wraps the function in another
230         function that will ensure the function is only called once.  If the
231         blueprint is registered a second time on the application, the
232         function passed is not called.
233         """
234 
235         def wrapper(state: BlueprintSetupState) -> None:
236             if state.first_registration:
237                 func(state)
238 
239         return self.record(update_wrapper(wrapper, func))
240 
241     def make_setup_state(
242         self, app: "Flask", options: dict, first_registration: bool = False
243     ) -> BlueprintSetupState:
244         """Creates an instance of :meth:`~flask.blueprints.BlueprintSetupState`
245         object that is later passed to the register callback functions.
246         Subclasses can override this to return a subclass of the setup state.
247         """
248         return BlueprintSetupState(self, app, options, first_registration)
249 
250     def register_blueprint(self, blueprint: "Blueprint", **options: t.Any) -> None:
251         """Register a :class:`~flask.Blueprint` on this blueprint. Keyword
252         arguments passed to this method will override the defaults set
253         on the blueprint.
254 
255         .. versionadded:: 2.0
256         """
257         self._blueprints.append((blueprint, options))
258 
259     def register(self, app: "Flask", options: dict) -> None:
260         """Called by :meth:`Flask.register_blueprint` to register all
261         views and callbacks registered on the blueprint with the
262         application. Creates a :class:`.BlueprintSetupState` and calls
263         each :meth:`record` callback with it.
264 
265         :param app: The application this blueprint is being registered
266             with.
267         :param options: Keyword arguments forwarded from
268             :meth:`~Flask.register_blueprint`.
269         :param first_registration: Whether this is the first time this
270             blueprint has been registered on the application.
271         """
272         first_registration = False
273 
274         if self.name in app.blueprints:
275             assert app.blueprints[self.name] is self, (
276                 "A name collision occurred between blueprints"
277                 f" {self!r} and {app.blueprints[self.name]!r}."
278                 f" Both share the same name {self.name!r}."
279                 f" Blueprints that are created on the fly need unique"
280                 f" names."
281             )
282         else:
283             app.blueprints[self.name] = self
284             first_registration = True
285 
286         self._got_registered_once = True
287         state = self.make_setup_state(app, options, first_registration)
288 
289         if self.has_static_folder:
290             state.add_url_rule(
291                 f"{self.static_url_path}/<path:filename>",
292                 view_func=self.send_static_file,
293                 endpoint="static",
294             )
295 
296         # Merge blueprint data into parent.
297         if first_registration:
298 
299             def extend(bp_dict, parent_dict):
300                 for key, values in bp_dict.items():
301                     key = self.name if key is None else f"{self.name}.{key}"
302 
303                     parent_dict[key].extend(values)
304 
305             for key, value in self.error_handler_spec.items():
306                 key = self.name if key is None else f"{self.name}.{key}"
307                 value = defaultdict(
308                     dict,
309                     {
310                         code: {
311                             exc_class: func for exc_class, func in code_values.items()
312                         }
313                         for code, code_values in value.items()
314                     },
315                 )
316                 app.error_handler_spec[key] = value
317 
318             for endpoint, func in self.view_functions.items():
319                 app.view_functions[endpoint] = func
320 
321             extend(self.before_request_funcs, app.before_request_funcs)
322             extend(self.after_request_funcs, app.after_request_funcs)
323             extend(
324                 self.teardown_request_funcs,
325                 app.teardown_request_funcs,
326             )
327             extend(self.url_default_functions, app.url_default_functions)
328             extend(self.url_value_preprocessors, app.url_value_preprocessors)
329             extend(self.template_context_processors, app.template_context_processors)
330 
331         for deferred in self.deferred_functions:
332             deferred(state)
333 
334         cli_resolved_group = options.get("cli_group", self.cli_group)
335 
336         if self.cli.commands:
337             if cli_resolved_group is None:
338                 app.cli.commands.update(self.cli.commands)
339             elif cli_resolved_group is _sentinel:
340                 self.cli.name = self.name
341                 app.cli.add_command(self.cli)
342             else:
343                 self.cli.name = cli_resolved_group
344                 app.cli.add_command(self.cli)
345 
346         for blueprint, bp_options in self._blueprints:
347             bp_options = bp_options.copy()
348             bp_url_prefix = bp_options.get("url_prefix")
349 
350             if bp_url_prefix is None:
351                 bp_url_prefix = blueprint.url_prefix
352 
353             if state.url_prefix is not None and bp_url_prefix is not None:
354                 bp_options["url_prefix"] = (
355                     state.url_prefix.rstrip("/") + "/" + bp_url_prefix.lstrip("/")
356                 )
357             else:
358                 bp_options["url_prefix"] = state.url_prefix
359 
360             bp_options["name_prefix"] = options.get("name_prefix", "") + self.name + "."
361             blueprint.register(app, bp_options)
362 
363     def add_url_rule(
364         self,
365         rule: str,
366         endpoint: t.Optional[str] = None,
367         view_func: t.Optional[t.Callable] = None,
368         provide_automatic_options: t.Optional[bool] = None,
369         **options: t.Any,
370     ) -> None:
371         """Like :meth:`Flask.add_url_rule` but for a blueprint.  The endpoint for
372         the :func:`url_for` function is prefixed with the name of the blueprint.
373         """
374         if endpoint and "." in endpoint:
375             raise ValueError("'endpoint' may not contain a dot '.' character.")
376 
377         if view_func and hasattr(view_func, "__name__") and "." in view_func.__name__:
378             raise ValueError("'view_func' name may not contain a dot '.' character.")
379 
380         self.record(
381             lambda s: s.add_url_rule(
382                 rule,
383                 endpoint,
384                 view_func,
385                 provide_automatic_options=provide_automatic_options,
386                 **options,
387             )
388         )

16 class DebugFilesKeyError(KeyError, AssertionError):
17     """Raised from request.files during debugging.  The idea is that it can
18     provide a better error message than just a generic KeyError/BadRequest.
19     """
20 
21     def __init__(self, request, key):
22         form_matches = request.form.getlist(key)
23         buf = [
24             f"You tried to access the file {key!r} in the request.files"
25             " dictionary but it does not exist. The mimetype for the"
26             f" request is {request.mimetype!r} instead of"
27             " 'multipart/form-data' which means that no file contents"
28             " were transmitted. To fix this error you should provide"
29             ' enctype="multipart/form-data" in your form.'
30         ]
31         if form_matches:
32             names = ", ".join(repr(x) for x in form_matches)
33             buf.append(
34                 "\n\nThe browser instead transmitted some file names. "
35                 f"This was submitted: {names}"
36             )
37         self.msg = "".join(buf)
38 
39     def __str__(self):
40         return self.msg

43 class FormDataRoutingRedirect(AssertionError):
44     """This exception is raised by Flask in debug mode if it detects a
45     redirect caused by the routing system when the request method is not
46     GET, HEAD or OPTIONS.  Reasoning: form data will be dropped.
47     """
48 
49     def __init__(self, request):
50         exc = request.routing_exception
51         buf = [
52             f"A request was sent to this URL ({request.url}) but a"
53             " redirect was issued automatically by the routing system"
54             f" to {exc.new_url!r}."
55         ]
56 
57         # In case just a slash was appended we can be extra helpful
58         if f"{request.base_url}/" == exc.new_url.split("?")[0]:
59             buf.append(
60                 "  The URL was defined with a trailing slash so Flask"
61                 " will automatically redirect to the URL with the"
62                 " trailing slash if it was accessed without one."
63             )
64 
65         buf.append(
66             "  Make sure to directly send your"
67             f" {request.method}-request to this URL since we can't make"
68             " browsers or HTTP clients redirect with form data reliably"
69             " or without user interaction."
70         )
71         buf.append("\n\nNote: this exception is only raised in debug mode")
72         AssertionError.__init__(self, "".join(buf).encode("utf-8"))

75 def attach_enctype_error_multidict(request):
76     """Since Flask 0.8 we're monkeypatching the files object in case a
77     request is detected that does not use multipart form data but the files
78     object is accessed.
79     """
80     oldcls = request.files.__class__
81 
82     class newcls(oldcls):
83         def __getitem__(self, key):
84             try:
85                 return oldcls.__getitem__(self, key)
86             except KeyError:
87                 if key not in request.form:
88                     raise
89                 raise DebugFilesKeyError(request, key)
90 
91     newcls.__name__ = oldcls.__name__
92     newcls.__module__ = oldcls.__module__
93     request.files.__class__ = newcls

96 def _dump_loader_info(loader) -> t.Generator:
97     yield f"class: {type(loader).__module__}.{type(loader).__name__}"
98     for key, value in sorted(loader.__dict__.items()):
99         if key.startswith("_"):
100             continue
101         if isinstance(value, (tuple, list)):
102             if not all(isinstance(x, str) for x in value):
103                 continue
104             yield f"{key}:"
105             for item in value:
106                 yield f"  - {item}"
107             continue
108         elif not isinstance(value, (str, int, float, bool)):
109             continue
110         yield f"{key}: {value!r}"
111 
112 
113 def explain_template_loading_attempts(app: Flask, template, attempts) -> None:
114     """This should help developers understand what failed"""
115     info = [f"Locating template {template!r}:"]
116     total_found = 0
117     blueprint = None
118     reqctx = _request_ctx_stack.top
119     if reqctx is not None and reqctx.request.blueprint is not None:
120         blueprint = reqctx.request.blueprint
121 
122     for idx, (loader, srcobj, triple) in enumerate(attempts):
123         if isinstance(srcobj, Flask):
124             src_info = f"application {srcobj.import_name!r}"
125         elif isinstance(srcobj, Blueprint):
126             src_info = f"blueprint {srcobj.name!r} ({srcobj.import_name})"
127         else:
128             src_info = repr(srcobj)
129 
130         info.append(f"{idx + 1:5}: trying loader of {src_info}")
131 
132         for line in _dump_loader_info(loader):
133             info.append(f"       {line}")
134 
135         if triple is None:
136             detail = "no match"
137         else:
138             detail = f"found ({triple[1] or '<string>'!r})"
139             total_found += 1
140         info.append(f"       -> {detail}")
141 
142     seems_fishy = False
143     if total_found == 0:
144         info.append("Error: the template could not be found.")
145         seems_fishy = True
146     elif total_found > 1:
147         info.append("Warning: multiple loaders returned a match for the template.")
148         seems_fishy = True
149 
150     if blueprint is not None and seems_fishy:
151         info.append(
152             "  The template was looked up from an endpoint that belongs"
153             f" to the blueprint {blueprint!r}."
154         )
155         info.append("  Maybe you did not place a template in the right folder?")
156         info.append("  See https://flask.palletsprojects.com/blueprints/#templates")
157 
158     app.logger.info("\n".join(info))
159

161 def explain_ignored_app_run() -> None:
162     if os.environ.get("WERKZEUG_RUN_MAIN") != "true":
163         warn(
164             Warning(
165                 "Silently ignoring app.run() because the application is"
166                 " run from the flask command line executable. Consider"
167                 ' putting app.run() behind an if __name__ == "__main__"'
168                 " guard to silence this warning."
169             ),
170             stacklevel=3,
171         )

65 def stream_with_context(
66     generator_or_function: t.Union[t.Generator, t.Callable]
67 ) -> t.Generator:
68     """Request contexts disappear when the response is started on the server.
69     This is done for efficiency reasons and to make it less likely to encounter
70     memory leaks with badly written WSGI middlewares.  The downside is that if
71     you are using streamed responses, the generator cannot access request bound
72     information any more.
73 
74     This function however can help you keep the context around for longer::
75 
76         from flask import stream_with_context, request, Response
77 
78         @app.route('/stream')
79         def streamed_response():
80             @stream_with_context
81             def generate():
82                 yield 'Hello '
83                 yield request.args['name']
84                 yield '!'
85             return Response(generate())
86 
87     Alternatively it can also be used around a specific generator::
88 
89         from flask import stream_with_context, request, Response
90 
91         @app.route('/stream')
92         def streamed_response():
93             def generate():
94                 yield 'Hello '
95                 yield request.args['name']
96                 yield '!'
97             return Response(stream_with_context(generate()))
98 
99     .. versionadded:: 0.9
100     """
101     try:
102         gen = iter(generator_or_function)  # type: ignore
103     except TypeError:
104 
105         def decorator(*args: t.Any, **kwargs: t.Any) -> t.Any:
106             gen = generator_or_function(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore
107             return stream_with_context(gen)
108 
109         return update_wrapper(decorator, generator_or_function)  # type: ignore
110 
111     def generator() -> t.Generator:
112         ctx = _request_ctx_stack.top
113         if ctx is None:
114             raise RuntimeError(
115                 "Attempted to stream with context but "
116                 "there was no context in the first place to keep around."
117             )
118         with ctx:
119             # Dummy sentinel.  Has to be inside the context block or we're
120             # not actually keeping the context around.
121             yield None
122 
123             # The try/finally is here so that if someone passes a WSGI level
124             # iterator in we're still running the cleanup logic.  Generators
125             # don't need that because they are closed on their destruction
126             # automatically.
127             try:
128                 yield from gen
129             finally:
130                 if hasattr(gen, "close"):
131                     gen.close()  # type: ignore
132 
133     # The trick is to start the generator.  Then the code execution runs until
134     # the first dummy None is yielded at which point the context was already
135     # pushed.  This item is discarded.  Then when the iteration continues the
136     # real generator is executed.
137     wrapped_g = generator()
138     next(wrapped_g)
139     return wrapped_g
140 
141 
142 def make_response(*args: t.Any) -> "Response":
143     """Sometimes it is necessary to set additional headers in a view.  Because
144     views do not have to return response objects but can return a value that
145     is converted into a response object by Flask itself, it becomes tricky to
146     add headers to it.  This function can be called instead of using a return
147     and you will get a response object which you can use to attach headers.
148 
149     If view looked like this and you want to add a new header::
150 
151         def index():
152             return render_template('index.html', foo=42)
153 
154     You can now do something like this::
155 
156         def index():
157             response = make_response(render_template('index.html', foo=42))
158             response.headers['X-Parachutes'] = 'parachutes are cool'
159             return response
160 
161     This function accepts the very same arguments you can return from a
162     view function.  This for example creates a response with a 404 error
163     code::
164 
165         response = make_response(render_template('not_found.html'), 404)
166 
167     The other use case of this function is to force the return value of a
168     view function into a response which is helpful with view
169     decorators::
170 
171         response = make_response(view_function())
172         response.headers['X-Parachutes'] = 'parachutes are cool'
173 
174     Internally this function does the following things:
175 
176     -   if no arguments are passed, it creates a new response argument
177     -   if one argument is passed, :meth:`flask.Flask.make_response`
178         is invoked with it.
179     -   if more than one argument is passed, the arguments are passed
180         to the :meth:`flask.Flask.make_response` function as tuple.
181 
182     .. versionadded:: 0.6
183     """
184     if not args:
185         return current_app.response_class()
186     if len(args) == 1:
187         args = args[0]
188     return current_app.make_response(args)
189

191 def url_for(endpoint: str, **values: t.Any) -> str:
192     """Generates a URL to the given endpoint with the method provided.
193 
194     Variable arguments that are unknown to the target endpoint are appended
195     to the generated URL as query arguments.  If the value of a query argument
196     is ``None``, the whole pair is skipped.  In case blueprints are active
197     you can shortcut references to the same blueprint by prefixing the
198     local endpoint with a dot (``.``).
199 
200     This will reference the index function local to the current blueprint::
201 
202         url_for('.index')
203 
204     See :ref:`url-building`.
205 
206     Configuration values ``APPLICATION_ROOT`` and ``SERVER_NAME`` are only used when
207     generating URLs outside of a request context.
208 
209     To integrate applications, :class:`Flask` has a hook to intercept URL build
210     errors through :attr:`Flask.url_build_error_handlers`.  The `url_for`
211     function results in a :exc:`~werkzeug.routing.BuildError` when the current
212     app does not have a URL for the given endpoint and values.  When it does, the
213     :data:`~flask.current_app` calls its :attr:`~Flask.url_build_error_handlers` if
214     it is not ``None``, which can return a string to use as the result of
215     `url_for` (instead of `url_for`'s default to raise the
216     :exc:`~werkzeug.routing.BuildError` exception) or re-raise the exception.
217     An example::
218 
219         def external_url_handler(error, endpoint, values):
220             "Looks up an external URL when `url_for` cannot build a URL."
221             # This is an example of hooking the build_error_handler.
222             # Here, lookup_url is some utility function you've built
223             # which looks up the endpoint in some external URL registry.
224             url = lookup_url(endpoint, **values)
225             if url is None:
226                 # External lookup did not have a URL.
227                 # Re-raise the BuildError, in context of original traceback.
228                 exc_type, exc_value, tb = sys.exc_info()
229                 if exc_value is error:
230                     raise exc_type(exc_value).with_traceback(tb)
231                 else:
232                     raise error
233             # url_for will use this result, instead of raising BuildError.
234             return url
235 
236         app.url_build_error_handlers.append(external_url_handler)
237 
238     Here, `error` is the instance of :exc:`~werkzeug.routing.BuildError`, and
239     `endpoint` and `values` are the arguments passed into `url_for`.  Note
240     that this is for building URLs outside the current application, and not for
241     handling 404 NotFound errors.
242 
243     .. versionadded:: 0.10
244        The `_scheme` parameter was added.
245 
246     .. versionadded:: 0.9
247        The `_anchor` and `_method` parameters were added.
248 
249     .. versionadded:: 0.9
250        Calls :meth:`Flask.handle_build_error` on
251        :exc:`~werkzeug.routing.BuildError`.
252 
253     :param endpoint: the endpoint of the URL (name of the function)
254     :param values: the variable arguments of the URL rule
255     :param _external: if set to ``True``, an absolute URL is generated. Server
256       address can be changed via ``SERVER_NAME`` configuration variable which
257       falls back to the `Host` header, then to the IP and port of the request.
258     :param _scheme: a string specifying the desired URL scheme. The `_external`
259       parameter must be set to ``True`` or a :exc:`ValueError` is raised. The default
260       behavior uses the same scheme as the current request, or
261       :data:`PREFERRED_URL_SCHEME` if no request context is available.
262       This also can be set to an empty string to build protocol-relative
263       URLs.
264     :param _anchor: if provided this is added as anchor to the URL.
265     :param _method: if provided this explicitly specifies an HTTP method.
266     """
267     appctx = _app_ctx_stack.top
268     reqctx = _request_ctx_stack.top
269 
270     if appctx is None:
271         raise RuntimeError(
272             "Attempted to generate a URL without the application context being"
273             " pushed. This has to be executed when application context is"
274             " available."
275         )
276 
277     # If request specific information is available we have some extra
278     # features that support "relative" URLs.
279     if reqctx is not None:
280         url_adapter = reqctx.url_adapter
281         blueprint_name = request.blueprint
282 
283         if endpoint[:1] == ".":
284             if blueprint_name is not None:
285                 endpoint = f"{blueprint_name}{endpoint}"
286             else:
287                 endpoint = endpoint[1:]
288 
289         external = values.pop("_external", False)
290 
291     # Otherwise go with the url adapter from the appctx and make
292     # the URLs external by default.
293     else:
294         url_adapter = appctx.url_adapter
295 
296         if url_adapter is None:
297             raise RuntimeError(
298                 "Application was not able to create a URL adapter for request"
299                 " independent URL generation. You might be able to fix this by"
300                 " setting the SERVER_NAME config variable."
301             )
302 
303         external = values.pop("_external", True)
304 
305     anchor = values.pop("_anchor", None)
306     method = values.pop("_method", None)
307     scheme = values.pop("_scheme", None)
308     appctx.app.inject_url_defaults(endpoint, values)
309 
310     # This is not the best way to deal with this but currently the
311     # underlying Werkzeug router does not support overriding the scheme on
312     # a per build call basis.
313     old_scheme = None
314     if scheme is not None:
315         if not external:
316             raise ValueError("When specifying _scheme, _external must be True")
317         old_scheme = url_adapter.url_scheme
318         url_adapter.url_scheme = scheme
319 
320     try:
321         try:
322             rv = url_adapter.build(
323                 endpoint, values, method=method, force_external=external
324             )
325         finally:
326             if old_scheme is not None:
327                 url_adapter.url_scheme = old_scheme
328     except BuildError as error:
329         # We need to inject the values again so that the app callback can
330         # deal with that sort of stuff.
331         values["_external"] = external
332         values["_anchor"] = anchor
333         values["_method"] = method
334         values["_scheme"] = scheme
335         return appctx.app.handle_url_build_error(error, endpoint, values)
336 
337     if anchor is not None:
338         rv += f"#{url_quote(anchor)}"
339     return rv

364 def flash(message: str, category: str = "message") -> None:
365     """Flashes a message to the next request.  In order to remove the
366     flashed message from the session and to display it to the user,
367     the template has to call :func:`get_flashed_messages`.
368 
369     .. versionchanged:: 0.3
370        `category` parameter added.
371 
372     :param message: the message to be flashed.
373     :param category: the category for the message.  The following values
374                      are recommended: ``'message'`` for any kind of message,
375                      ``'error'`` for errors, ``'info'`` for information
376                      messages and ``'warning'`` for warnings.  However any
377                      kind of string can be used as category.
378     """
379     # Original implementation:
380     #
381     #     session.setdefault('_flashes', []).append((category, message))
382     #
383     # This assumed that changes made to mutable structures in the session are
384     # always in sync with the session object, which is not true for session
385     # implementations that use external storage for keeping their keys/values.
386     flashes = session.get("_flashes", [])
387     flashes.append((category, message))
388     session["_flashes"] = flashes
389     message_flashed.send(
390         current_app._get_current_object(),  # type: ignore
391         message=message,
392         category=category,
393     )
394 
395 
396 def get_flashed_messages(
397     with_categories: bool = False, category_filter: t.Iterable[str] = ()
398 ) -> t.Union[t.List[str], t.List[t.Tuple[str, str]]]:
399     """Pulls all flashed messages from the session and returns them.
400     Further calls in the same request to the function will return
401     the same messages.  By default just the messages are returned,
402     but when `with_categories` is set to ``True``, the return value will
403     be a list of tuples in the form ``(category, message)`` instead.
404 
405     Filter the flashed messages to one or more categories by providing those
406     categories in `category_filter`.  This allows rendering categories in
407     separate html blocks.  The `with_categories` and `category_filter`
408     arguments are distinct:
409 
410     * `with_categories` controls whether categories are returned with message
411       text (``True`` gives a tuple, where ``False`` gives just the message text).
412     * `category_filter` filters the messages down to only those matching the
413       provided categories.
414 
415     See :doc:`/patterns/flashing` for examples.
416 
417     .. versionchanged:: 0.3
418        `with_categories` parameter added.
419 
420     .. versionchanged:: 0.9
421         `category_filter` parameter added.
422 
423     :param with_categories: set to ``True`` to also receive categories.
424     :param category_filter: filter of categories to limit return values.  Only
425                             categories in the list will be returned.
426     """
427     flashes = _request_ctx_stack.top.flashes
428     if flashes is None:
429         _request_ctx_stack.top.flashes = flashes = (
430             session.pop("_flashes") if "_flashes" in session else []
431         )
432     if category_filter:
433         flashes = list(filter(lambda f: f[0] in category_filter, flashes))
434     if not with_categories:
435         return [x[1] for x in flashes]
436     return flashes
437
