132 class OneVsRestClassifier(BaseEstimator, ClassifierMixin, MetaEstimatorMixin,
133                           MultiOutputMixin):
134     """One-vs-the-rest (OvR) multiclass/multilabel strategy
135 
136     Also known as one-vs-all, this strategy consists in fitting one classifier
137     per class. For each classifier, the class is fitted against all the other
138     classes. In addition to its computational efficiency (only `n_classes`
139     classifiers are needed), one advantage of this approach is its
140     interpretability. Since each class is represented by one and one classifier
141     only, it is possible to gain knowledge about the class by inspecting its
142     corresponding classifier. This is the most commonly used strategy for
143     multiclass classification and is a fair default choice.
144 
145     This strategy can also be used for multilabel learning, where a classifier
146     is used to predict multiple labels for instance, by fitting on a 2-d matrix
147     in which cell [i, j] is 1 if sample i has label j and 0 otherwise.
148 
149     In the multilabel learning literature, OvR is also known as the binary
150     relevance method.
151 
152     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <ovr_classification>`.
153 
154     Parameters
155     ----------
156     estimator : estimator object
157         An estimator object implementing `fit` and one of `decision_function`
158         or `predict_proba`.
159 
160     n_jobs : int or None, optional (default=None)
161         The number of jobs to use for the computation.
162         ``None`` means 1 unless in a :obj:`joblib.parallel_backend` context.
163         ``-1`` means using all processors. See :term:`Glossary <n_jobs>`
164         for more details.
165 
166     Attributes
167     ----------
168     estimators_ : list of `n_classes` estimators
169         Estimators used for predictions.
170 
171     classes_ : array, shape = [`n_classes`]
172         Class labels.
173     label_binarizer_ : LabelBinarizer object
174         Object used to transform multiclass labels to binary labels and
175         vice-versa.
176     multilabel_ : boolean
177         Whether a OneVsRestClassifier is a multilabel classifier.
178     """
179     def __init__(self, estimator, n_jobs=None):
180         self.estimator = estimator
181         self.n_jobs = n_jobs
182 
183     def fit(self, X, y):
184         """Fit underlying estimators.
185 
186         Parameters
187         ----------
188         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
189             Data.
190 
191         y : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, ], [n_samples, n_classes]
192             Multi-class targets. An indicator matrix turns on multilabel
193             classification.
194 
195         Returns
196         -------
197         self
198         """
199         # A sparse LabelBinarizer, with sparse_output=True, has been shown to
200         # outperform or match a dense label binarizer in all cases and has also
201         # resulted in less or equal memory consumption in the fit_ovr function
202         # overall.
203         self.label_binarizer_ = LabelBinarizer(sparse_output=True)
204         Y = self.label_binarizer_.fit_transform(y)
205         Y = Y.tocsc()
206         self.classes_ = self.label_binarizer_.classes_
207         columns = (col.toarray().ravel() for col in Y.T)
208         # In cases where individual estimators are very fast to train setting
209         # n_jobs > 1 in can results in slower performance due to the overhead
210         # of spawning threads.  See joblib issue #112.
211         self.estimators_ = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(delayed(_fit_binary)(
212             self.estimator, X, column, classes=[
213                 "not %s" % self.label_binarizer_.classes_[i],
214                 self.label_binarizer_.classes_[i]])
215             for i, column in enumerate(columns))
216 
217         return self
218 
219     @if_delegate_has_method('estimator')
220     def partial_fit(self, X, y, classes=None):
221         """Partially fit underlying estimators
222 
223         Should be used when memory is inefficient to train all data.
224         Chunks of data can be passed in several iteration.
225 
226         Parameters
227         ----------
228         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
229             Data.
230 
231         y : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, ], [n_samples, n_classes]
232             Multi-class targets. An indicator matrix turns on multilabel
233             classification.
234 
235         classes : array, shape (n_classes, )
236             Classes across all calls to partial_fit.
237             Can be obtained via `np.unique(y_all)`, where y_all is the
238             target vector of the entire dataset.
239             This argument is only required in the first call of partial_fit
240             and can be omitted in the subsequent calls.
241 
242         Returns
243         -------
244         self
245         """
246         if _check_partial_fit_first_call(self, classes):
247             if not hasattr(self.estimator, "partial_fit"):
248                 raise ValueError(("Base estimator {0}, doesn't have "
249                                  "partial_fit method").format(self.estimator))
250             self.estimators_ = [clone(self.estimator) for _ in range
251                                 (self.n_classes_)]
252 
253             # A sparse LabelBinarizer, with sparse_output=True, has been
254             # shown to outperform or match a dense label binarizer in all
255             # cases and has also resulted in less or equal memory consumption
256             # in the fit_ovr function overall.
257             self.label_binarizer_ = LabelBinarizer(sparse_output=True)
258             self.label_binarizer_.fit(self.classes_)
259 
260         if len(np.setdiff1d(y, self.classes_)):
261             raise ValueError(("Mini-batch contains {0} while classes " +
262                              "must be subset of {1}").format(np.unique(y),
263                                                              self.classes_))
264 
265         Y = self.label_binarizer_.transform(y)
266         Y = Y.tocsc()
267         columns = (col.toarray().ravel() for col in Y.T)
268 
269         self.estimators_ = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
270             delayed(_partial_fit_binary)(estimator, X, column)
271             for estimator, column in zip(self.estimators_, columns))
272 
273         return self
274 
275     def predict(self, X):
276         """Predict multi-class targets using underlying estimators.
277 
278         Parameters
279         ----------
280         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
281             Data.
282 
283         Returns
284         -------
285         y : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, ], [n_samples, n_classes].
286             Predicted multi-class targets.
287         """
288         check_is_fitted(self)
289 
290         n_samples = _num_samples(X)
291         if self.label_binarizer_.y_type_ == "multiclass":
292             maxima = np.empty(n_samples, dtype=float)
293             maxima.fill(-np.inf)
294             argmaxima = np.zeros(n_samples, dtype=int)
295             for i, e in enumerate(self.estimators_):
296                 pred = _predict_binary(e, X)
297                 np.maximum(maxima, pred, out=maxima)
298                 argmaxima[maxima == pred] = i
299             return self.classes_[argmaxima]
300         else:
301             if (hasattr(self.estimators_[0], "decision_function") and
302                     is_classifier(self.estimators_[0])):
303                 thresh = 0
304             else:
305                 thresh = .5
306             indices = array.array('i')
307             indptr = array.array('i', [0])
308             for e in self.estimators_:
309                 indices.extend(np.where(_predict_binary(e, X) > thresh)[0])
310                 indptr.append(len(indices))
311             data = np.ones(len(indices), dtype=int)
312             indicator = sp.csc_matrix((data, indices, indptr),
313                                       shape=(n_samples, len(self.estimators_)))
314             return self.label_binarizer_.inverse_transform(indicator)
315 
316     @if_delegate_has_method(['_first_estimator', 'estimator'])
317     def predict_proba(self, X):
318         """Probability estimates.
319 
320         The returned estimates for all classes are ordered by label of classes.
321 
322         Note that in the multilabel case, each sample can have any number of
323         labels. This returns the marginal probability that the given sample has
324         the label in question. For example, it is entirely consistent that two
325         labels both have a 90% probability of applying to a given sample.
326 
327         In the single label multiclass case, the rows of the returned matrix
328         sum to 1.
329 
330         Parameters
331         ----------
332         X : array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
333 
334         Returns
335         -------
336         T : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_classes]
337             Returns the probability of the sample for each class in the model,
338             where classes are ordered as they are in `self.classes_`.
339         """
340         check_is_fitted(self)
341         # Y[i, j] gives the probability that sample i has the label j.
342         # In the multi-label case, these are not disjoint.
343         Y = np.array([e.predict_proba(X)[:, 1] for e in self.estimators_]).T
344 
345         if len(self.estimators_) == 1:
346             # Only one estimator, but we still want to return probabilities
347             # for two classes.
348             Y = np.concatenate(((1 - Y), Y), axis=1)
349 
350         if not self.multilabel_:
351             # Then, probabilities should be normalized to 1.
352             Y /= np.sum(Y, axis=1)[:, np.newaxis]
353         return Y
354 
355     @if_delegate_has_method(['_first_estimator', 'estimator'])
356     def decision_function(self, X):
357         """Returns the distance of each sample from the decision boundary for
358         each class. This can only be used with estimators which implement the
359         decision_function method.
360 
361         Parameters
362         ----------
363         X : array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
364 
365         Returns
366         -------
367         T : array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_classes]
368         """
369         check_is_fitted(self)
370         if len(self.estimators_) == 1:
371             return self.estimators_[0].decision_function(X)
372         return np.array([est.decision_function(X).ravel()
373                          for est in self.estimators_]).T
374 
375     @property
376     def multilabel_(self):
377         """Whether this is a multilabel classifier"""
378         return self.label_binarizer_.y_type_.startswith('multilabel')
379 
380     @property
381     def n_classes_(self):
382         return len(self.classes_)
383 
384     @property
385     def coef_(self):
386         check_is_fitted(self)
387         if not hasattr(self.estimators_[0], "coef_"):
388             raise AttributeError(
389                 "Base estimator doesn't have a coef_ attribute.")
390         coefs = [e.coef_ for e in self.estimators_]
391         if sp.issparse(coefs[0]):
392             return sp.vstack(coefs)
393         return np.vstack(coefs)
394 
395     @property
396     def intercept_(self):
397         check_is_fitted(self)
398         if not hasattr(self.estimators_[0], "intercept_"):
399             raise AttributeError(
400                 "Base estimator doesn't have an intercept_ attribute.")
401         return np.array([e.intercept_.ravel() for e in self.estimators_])
402 
403     @property
404     def _pairwise(self):
405         """Indicate if wrapped estimator is using a precomputed Gram matrix"""
406         return getattr(self.estimator, "_pairwise", False)
407 
408     @property
409     def _first_estimator(self):
410         return self.estimators_[0]
411 
412 
413 def _fit_ovo_binary(estimator, X, y, i, j):
414     """Fit a single binary estimator (one-vs-one)."""
415     cond = np.logical_or(y == i, y == j)
416     y = y[cond]
417     y_binary = np.empty(y.shape, np.int)
418     y_binary[y == i] = 0
419     y_binary[y == j] = 1
420     indcond = np.arange(X.shape[0])[cond]
421     return _fit_binary(estimator,
422                        _safe_split(estimator, X, None, indices=indcond)[0],
423                        y_binary, classes=[i, j]), indcond
424 
425 
426 def _partial_fit_ovo_binary(estimator, X, y, i, j):
427     """Partially fit a single binary estimator(one-vs-one)."""
428 
429     cond = np.logical_or(y == i, y == j)
430     y = y[cond]
431     if len(y) != 0:
432         y_binary = np.zeros_like(y)
433         y_binary[y == j] = 1
434         return _partial_fit_binary(estimator, X[cond], y_binary)
435     return estimator
436

438 class OneVsOneClassifier(BaseEstimator, ClassifierMixin, MetaEstimatorMixin):
439     """One-vs-one multiclass strategy
440 
441     This strategy consists in fitting one classifier per class pair.
442     At prediction time, the class which received the most votes is selected.
443     Since it requires to fit `n_classes * (n_classes - 1) / 2` classifiers,
444     this method is usually slower than one-vs-the-rest, due to its
445     O(n_classes^2) complexity. However, this method may be advantageous for
446     algorithms such as kernel algorithms which don't scale well with
447     `n_samples`. This is because each individual learning problem only involves
448     a small subset of the data whereas, with one-vs-the-rest, the complete
449     dataset is used `n_classes` times.
450 
451     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <ovo_classification>`.
452 
453     Parameters
454     ----------
455     estimator : estimator object
456         An estimator object implementing `fit` and one of `decision_function`
457         or `predict_proba`.
458 
459     n_jobs : int or None, optional (default=None)
460         The number of jobs to use for the computation.
461         ``None`` means 1 unless in a :obj:`joblib.parallel_backend` context.
462         ``-1`` means using all processors. See :term:`Glossary <n_jobs>`
463         for more details.
464 
465     Attributes
466     ----------
467     estimators_ : list of ``n_classes * (n_classes - 1) / 2`` estimators
468         Estimators used for predictions.
469 
470     classes_ : numpy array of shape [n_classes]
471         Array containing labels.
472 
473     pairwise_indices_ : list, length = ``len(estimators_)``, or ``None``
474         Indices of samples used when training the estimators.
475         ``None`` when ``estimator`` does not have ``_pairwise`` attribute.
476     """
477 
478     def __init__(self, estimator, n_jobs=None):
479         self.estimator = estimator
480         self.n_jobs = n_jobs
481 
482     def fit(self, X, y):
483         """Fit underlying estimators.
484 
485         Parameters
486         ----------
487         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
488             Data.
489 
490         y : array-like, shape = [n_samples]
491             Multi-class targets.
492 
493         Returns
494         -------
495         self
496         """
497         X, y = check_X_y(X, y, accept_sparse=['csr', 'csc'])
498         check_classification_targets(y)
499 
500         self.classes_ = np.unique(y)
501         if len(self.classes_) == 1:
502             raise ValueError("OneVsOneClassifier can not be fit when only one"
503                              " class is present.")
504         n_classes = self.classes_.shape[0]
505         estimators_indices = list(zip(*(Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
506             delayed(_fit_ovo_binary)
507             (self.estimator, X, y, self.classes_[i], self.classes_[j])
508             for i in range(n_classes) for j in range(i + 1, n_classes)))))
509 
510         self.estimators_ = estimators_indices[0]
511         self.pairwise_indices_ = (
512             estimators_indices[1] if self._pairwise else None)
513 
514         return self
515 
516     @if_delegate_has_method(delegate='estimator')
517     def partial_fit(self, X, y, classes=None):
518         """Partially fit underlying estimators
519 
520         Should be used when memory is inefficient to train all data. Chunks
521         of data can be passed in several iteration, where the first call
522         should have an array of all target variables.
523 
524 
525         Parameters
526         ----------
527         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
528             Data.
529 
530         y : array-like, shape = [n_samples]
531             Multi-class targets.
532 
533         classes : array, shape (n_classes, )
534             Classes across all calls to partial_fit.
535             Can be obtained via `np.unique(y_all)`, where y_all is the
536             target vector of the entire dataset.
537             This argument is only required in the first call of partial_fit
538             and can be omitted in the subsequent calls.
539 
540         Returns
541         -------
542         self
543         """
544         if _check_partial_fit_first_call(self, classes):
545             self.estimators_ = [clone(self.estimator) for _ in
546                                 range(self.n_classes_ *
547                                       (self.n_classes_ - 1) // 2)]
548 
549         if len(np.setdiff1d(y, self.classes_)):
550             raise ValueError("Mini-batch contains {0} while it "
551                              "must be subset of {1}".format(np.unique(y),
552                                                             self.classes_))
553 
554         X, y = check_X_y(X, y, accept_sparse=['csr', 'csc'])
555         check_classification_targets(y)
556         combinations = itertools.combinations(range(self.n_classes_), 2)
557         self.estimators_ = Parallel(
558             n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
559                 delayed(_partial_fit_ovo_binary)(
560                     estimator, X, y, self.classes_[i], self.classes_[j])
561                 for estimator, (i, j) in zip(self.estimators_,
562                                               (combinations)))
563 
564         self.pairwise_indices_ = None
565 
566         return self
567 
568     def predict(self, X):
569         """Estimate the best class label for each sample in X.
570 
571         This is implemented as ``argmax(decision_function(X), axis=1)`` which
572         will return the label of the class with most votes by estimators
573         predicting the outcome of a decision for each possible class pair.
574 
575         Parameters
576         ----------
577         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
578             Data.
579 
580         Returns
581         -------
582         y : numpy array of shape [n_samples]
583             Predicted multi-class targets.
584         """
585         Y = self.decision_function(X)
586         if self.n_classes_ == 2:
587             return self.classes_[(Y > 0).astype(np.int)]
588         return self.classes_[Y.argmax(axis=1)]
589 
590     def decision_function(self, X):
591         """Decision function for the OneVsOneClassifier.
592 
593         The decision values for the samples are computed by adding the
594         normalized sum of pair-wise classification confidence levels to the
595         votes in order to disambiguate between the decision values when the
596         votes for all the classes are equal leading to a tie.
597 
598         Parameters
599         ----------
600         X : array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
601 
602         Returns
603         -------
604         Y : array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_classes]
605         """
606         check_is_fitted(self)
607 
608         indices = self.pairwise_indices_
609         if indices is None:
610             Xs = [X] * len(self.estimators_)
611         else:
612             Xs = [X[:, idx] for idx in indices]
613 
614         predictions = np.vstack([est.predict(Xi)
615                                  for est, Xi in zip(self.estimators_, Xs)]).T
616         confidences = np.vstack([_predict_binary(est, Xi)
617                                  for est, Xi in zip(self.estimators_, Xs)]).T
618         Y = _ovr_decision_function(predictions,
619                                    confidences, len(self.classes_))
620         if self.n_classes_ == 2:
621             return Y[:, 1]
622         return Y
623 
624     @property
625     def n_classes_(self):
626         return len(self.classes_)
627 
628     @property
629     def _pairwise(self):
630         """Indicate if wrapped estimator is using a precomputed Gram matrix"""
631         return getattr(self.estimator, "_pairwise", False)
632

634 class OutputCodeClassifier(BaseEstimator, ClassifierMixin, MetaEstimatorMixin):
635     """(Error-Correcting) Output-Code multiclass strategy
636 
637     Output-code based strategies consist in representing each class with a
638     binary code (an array of 0s and 1s). At fitting time, one binary
639     classifier per bit in the code book is fitted.  At prediction time, the
640     classifiers are used to project new points in the class space and the class
641     closest to the points is chosen. The main advantage of these strategies is
642     that the number of classifiers used can be controlled by the user, either
643     for compressing the model (0 < code_size < 1) or for making the model more
644     robust to errors (code_size > 1). See the documentation for more details.
645 
646     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <ecoc>`.
647 
648     Parameters
649     ----------
650     estimator : estimator object
651         An estimator object implementing `fit` and one of `decision_function`
652         or `predict_proba`.
653 
654     code_size : float
655         Percentage of the number of classes to be used to create the code book.
656         A number between 0 and 1 will require fewer classifiers than
657         one-vs-the-rest. A number greater than 1 will require more classifiers
658         than one-vs-the-rest.
659 
660     random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, optional, default: None
661         The generator used to initialize the codebook.  If int, random_state is
662         the seed used by the random number generator; If RandomState instance,
663         random_state is the random number generator; If None, the random number
664         generator is the RandomState instance used by `np.random`.
665 
666     n_jobs : int or None, optional (default=None)
667         The number of jobs to use for the computation.
668         ``None`` means 1 unless in a :obj:`joblib.parallel_backend` context.
669         ``-1`` means using all processors. See :term:`Glossary <n_jobs>`
670         for more details.
671 
672     Attributes
673     ----------
674     estimators_ : list of `int(n_classes * code_size)` estimators
675         Estimators used for predictions.
676 
677     classes_ : numpy array of shape [n_classes]
678         Array containing labels.
679 
680     code_book_ : numpy array of shape [n_classes, code_size]
681         Binary array containing the code of each class.
682 
683     References
684     ----------
685 
686     .. [1] "Solving multiclass learning problems via error-correcting output
687        codes",
688        Dietterich T., Bakiri G.,
689        Journal of Artificial Intelligence Research 2,
690        1995.
691 
692     .. [2] "The error coding method and PICTs",
693        James G., Hastie T.,
694        Journal of Computational and Graphical statistics 7,
695        1998.
696 
697     .. [3] "The Elements of Statistical Learning",
698        Hastie T., Tibshirani R., Friedman J., page 606 (second-edition)
699        2008.
700     """
701 
702     def __init__(self, estimator, code_size=1.5, random_state=None,
703                  n_jobs=None):
704         self.estimator = estimator
705         self.code_size = code_size
706         self.random_state = random_state
707         self.n_jobs = n_jobs
708 
709     def fit(self, X, y):
710         """Fit underlying estimators.
711 
712         Parameters
713         ----------
714         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
715             Data.
716 
717         y : numpy array of shape [n_samples]
718             Multi-class targets.
719 
720         Returns
721         -------
722         self
723         """
724         X, y = check_X_y(X, y)
725         if self.code_size <= 0:
726             raise ValueError("code_size should be greater than 0, got {0}"
727                              "".format(self.code_size))
728 
729         _check_estimator(self.estimator)
730         random_state = check_random_state(self.random_state)
731         check_classification_targets(y)
732 
733         self.classes_ = np.unique(y)
734         n_classes = self.classes_.shape[0]
735         code_size_ = int(n_classes * self.code_size)
736 
737         # FIXME: there are more elaborate methods than generating the codebook
738         # randomly.
739         self.code_book_ = random_state.random_sample((n_classes, code_size_))
740         self.code_book_[self.code_book_ > 0.5] = 1
741 
742         if hasattr(self.estimator, "decision_function"):
743             self.code_book_[self.code_book_ != 1] = -1
744         else:
745             self.code_book_[self.code_book_ != 1] = 0
746 
747         classes_index = {c: i for i, c in enumerate(self.classes_)}
748 
749         Y = np.array([self.code_book_[classes_index[y[i]]]
750                       for i in range(X.shape[0])], dtype=np.int)
751 
752         self.estimators_ = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
753             delayed(_fit_binary)(self.estimator, X, Y[:, i])
754             for i in range(Y.shape[1]))
755 
756         return self
757 
758     def predict(self, X):
759         """Predict multi-class targets using underlying estimators.
760 
761         Parameters
762         ----------
763         X : (sparse) array-like, shape = [n_samples, n_features]
764             Data.
765 
766         Returns
767         -------
768         y : numpy array of shape [n_samples]
769             Predicted multi-class targets.
770         """
771         check_is_fitted(self)
772         X = check_array(X)
773         Y = np.array([_predict_binary(e, X) for e in self.estimators_]).T
774         pred = euclidean_distances(Y, self.code_book_).argmin(axis=1)
775         return self.classes_[pred]

1 """
2 This module implements multioutput regression and classification.
3 
4 The estimators provided in this module are meta-estimators: they require
5 a base estimator to be provided in their constructor. The meta-estimator
6 extends single output estimators to multioutput estimators.
7 """

17 import numpy as np
18 import scipy.sparse as sp
19 from joblib import Parallel, delayed
20 
21 from abc import ABCMeta, abstractmethod
22 from .base import BaseEstimator, clone, MetaEstimatorMixin
23 from .base import RegressorMixin, ClassifierMixin, is_classifier
24 from .model_selection import cross_val_predict
25 from .utils import check_array, check_X_y, check_random_state
26 from .utils.fixes import parallel_helper
27 from .utils.metaestimators import if_delegate_has_method
28 from .utils.validation import check_is_fitted, has_fit_parameter
29 from .utils.multiclass import check_classification_targets
30 
31 __all__ = ["MultiOutputRegressor", "MultiOutputClassifier",
32            "ClassifierChain", "RegressorChain"]
33 
34 
35 def _fit_estimator(estimator, X, y, sample_weight=None):
36     estimator = clone(estimator)
37     if sample_weight is not None:
38         estimator.fit(X, y, sample_weight=sample_weight)
39     else:
40         estimator.fit(X, y)
41     return estimator
42 
43 
44 def _partial_fit_estimator(estimator, X, y, classes=None, sample_weight=None,
45                            first_time=True):
46     if first_time:
47         estimator = clone(estimator)
48 
49     if sample_weight is not None:
50         if classes is not None:
51             estimator.partial_fit(X, y, classes=classes,
52                                   sample_weight=sample_weight)
53         else:
54             estimator.partial_fit(X, y, sample_weight=sample_weight)
55     else:
56         if classes is not None:
57             estimator.partial_fit(X, y, classes=classes)
58         else:
59             estimator.partial_fit(X, y)
60     return estimator
61

63 class MultiOutputEstimator(BaseEstimator, MetaEstimatorMixin,
64                            metaclass=ABCMeta):
65     @abstractmethod
66     def __init__(self, estimator, n_jobs=None):
67         self.estimator = estimator
68         self.n_jobs = n_jobs
69 
70     @if_delegate_has_method('estimator')
71     def partial_fit(self, X, y, classes=None, sample_weight=None):
72         """Incrementally fit the model to data.
73         Fit a separate model for each output variable.
74 
75         Parameters
76         ----------
77         X : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
78             Data.
79 
80         y : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_outputs)
81             Multi-output targets.
82 
83         classes : list of numpy arrays, shape (n_outputs)
84             Each array is unique classes for one output in str/int
85             Can be obtained by via
86             ``[np.unique(y[:, i]) for i in range(y.shape[1])]``, where y is the
87             target matrix of the entire dataset.
88             This argument is required for the first call to partial_fit
89             and can be omitted in the subsequent calls.
90             Note that y doesn't need to contain all labels in `classes`.
91 
92         sample_weight : array-like, shape = (n_samples) or None
93             Sample weights. If None, then samples are equally weighted.
94             Only supported if the underlying regressor supports sample
95             weights.
96 
97         Returns
98         -------
99         self : object
100         """
101         X, y = check_X_y(X, y,
102                          multi_output=True,
103                          accept_sparse=True)
104 
105         if y.ndim == 1:
106             raise ValueError("y must have at least two dimensions for "
107                              "multi-output regression but has only one.")
108 
109         if (sample_weight is not None and
110                 not has_fit_parameter(self.estimator, 'sample_weight')):
111             raise ValueError("Underlying estimator does not support"
112                              " sample weights.")
113 
114         first_time = not hasattr(self, 'estimators_')
115 
116         self.estimators_ = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
117             delayed(_partial_fit_estimator)(
118                 self.estimators_[i] if not first_time else self.estimator,
119                 X, y[:, i],
120                 classes[i] if classes is not None else None,
121                 sample_weight, first_time) for i in range(y.shape[1]))
122         return self
123 
124     def fit(self, X, y, sample_weight=None):
125         """ Fit the model to data.
126         Fit a separate model for each output variable.
127 
128         Parameters
129         ----------
130         X : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
131             Data.
132 
133         y : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_outputs)
134             Multi-output targets. An indicator matrix turns on multilabel
135             estimation.
136 
137         sample_weight : array-like, shape = (n_samples) or None
138             Sample weights. If None, then samples are equally weighted.
139             Only supported if the underlying regressor supports sample
140             weights.
141 
142         Returns
143         -------
144         self : object
145         """
146 
147         if not hasattr(self.estimator, "fit"):
148             raise ValueError("The base estimator should implement"
149                              " a fit method")
150 
151         X, y = check_X_y(X, y,
152                          multi_output=True,
153                          accept_sparse=True)
154 
155         if is_classifier(self):
156             check_classification_targets(y)
157 
158         if y.ndim == 1:
159             raise ValueError("y must have at least two dimensions for "
160                              "multi-output regression but has only one.")
161 
162         if (sample_weight is not None and
163                 not has_fit_parameter(self.estimator, 'sample_weight')):
164             raise ValueError("Underlying estimator does not support"
165                              " sample weights.")
166 
167         self.estimators_ = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
168             delayed(_fit_estimator)(
169                 self.estimator, X, y[:, i], sample_weight)
170             for i in range(y.shape[1]))
171         return self
172 
173     def predict(self, X):
174         """Predict multi-output variable using a model
175          trained for each target variable.
176 
177         Parameters
178         ----------
179         X : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
180             Data.
181 
182         Returns
183         -------
184         y : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_outputs)
185             Multi-output targets predicted across multiple predictors.
186             Note: Separate models are generated for each predictor.
187         """
188         check_is_fitted(self)
189         if not hasattr(self.estimator, "predict"):
190             raise ValueError("The base estimator should implement"
191                              " a predict method")
192 
193         X = check_array(X, accept_sparse=True)
194 
195         y = Parallel(n_jobs=self.n_jobs)(
196             delayed(parallel_helper)(e, 'predict', X)
197             for e in self.estimators_)
198 
199         return np.asarray(y).T
200 
201     def _more_tags(self):
202         return {'multioutput_only': True}
203 
204 
205 class MultiOutputRegressor(MultiOutputEstimator, RegressorMixin):
206     """Multi target regression
207 
208     This strategy consists of fitting one regressor per target. This is a
209     simple strategy for extending regressors that do not natively support
210     multi-target regression.
211 
212     Parameters
213     ----------
214     estimator : estimator object
215         An estimator object implementing `fit` and `predict`.
216 
217     n_jobs : int or None, optional (default=None)
218         The number of jobs to run in parallel for `fit`.
219         ``None`` means 1 unless in a :obj:`joblib.parallel_backend` context.
220         ``-1`` means using all processors. See :term:`Glossary <n_jobs>`
221         for more details.
222 
223         When individual estimators are fast to train or predict
224         using `n_jobs>1` can result in slower performance due
225         to the overhead of spawning processes.
226 
227     Attributes
228     ----------
229     estimators_ : list of ``n_output`` estimators
230         Estimators used for predictions.
231     """
232 
233     def __init__(self, estimator, n_jobs=None):
234         super().__init__(estimator, n_jobs)
235 
236     @if_delegate_has_method('estimator')
237     def partial_fit(self, X, y, sample_weight=None):
238         """Incrementally fit the model to data.
239         Fit a separate model for each output variable.
240 
241         Parameters
242         ----------
243         X : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
244             Data.
245 
246         y : (sparse) array-like, shape (n_samples, n_outputs)
247             Multi-output targets.
248 
249         sample_weight : array-like, shape = (n_samples) or None
250             Sample weights. If None, then samples are equally weighted.
251             Only supported if the underlying regressor supports sample
252             weights.
253 
254         Returns
255         -------
256         self : object
257         """
258         super().partial_fit(
259             X, y, sample_weight=sample_weight)
260 
261     # XXX Remove this method in 0.23
262     def score(self, X, y, sample_weight=None):
263         """Returns the coefficient of determination R^2 of the prediction.
264 
265         The coefficient R^2 is defined as (1 - u/v), where u is the residual
266         sum of squares ((y_true - y_pred) ** 2).sum() and v is the regression
267         sum of squares ((y_true - y_true.mean()) ** 2).sum().
268         Best possible score is 1.0 and it can be negative (because the
269         model can be arbitrarily worse). A constant model that always
270         predicts the expected value of y, disregarding the input features,
271         would get a R^2 score of 0.0.
272 
273         Notes
274         -----
275         R^2 is calculated by weighting all the targets equally using
276         `multioutput='uniform_average'`.
277 
278         Parameters
279         ----------
280         X : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
281             Test samples.
282 
283         y : array-like, shape (n_samples) or (n_samples, n_outputs)
284             True values for X.
285 
286         sample_weight : array-like, shape [n_samples], optional
287             Sample weights.
288 
289         Returns
290         -------
291         score : float
292             R^2 of self.predict(X) wrt. y.
293         """
294         # XXX remove in 0.19 when r2_score default for multioutput changes
295         from .metrics import r2_score
296         return r2_score(y, self.predict(X), sample_weight=sample_weight,
297                         multioutput='uniform_average')
298 
299 
300 class MultiOutputClassifier(MultiOutputEstimator, ClassifierMixin):
301     """Multi target classification
302 
303     This strategy consists of fitting one classifier per target. This is a
304     simple strategy for extending classifiers that do not natively support
305     multi-target classification
306 
307     Parameters
308     ----------
309     estimator : estimator object
310         An estimator object implementing `fit`, `score` and `predict_proba`.
311 
312     n_jobs : int or None, optional (default=None)
313         The number of jobs to use for the computation.
314         It does each target variable in y in parallel.
315         ``None`` means 1 unless in a :obj:`joblib.parallel_backend` context.
316         ``-1`` means using all processors. See :term:`Glossary <n_jobs>`
317         for more details.
318 
319     Attributes
320     ----------
321     estimators_ : list of ``n_output`` estimators
322         Estimators used for predictions.
323     """
324 
325     def __init__(self, estimator, n_jobs=None):
326         super().__init__(estimator, n_jobs)
327 
328     def predict_proba(self, X):
329         """Probability estimates.
330         Returns prediction probabilities for each class of each output.
331 
332         This method will raise a ``ValueError`` if any of the
333         estimators do not have ``predict_proba``.
334 
335         Parameters
336         ----------
337         X : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
338             Data
339 
340         Returns
341         -------
342         p : array of shape = [n_samples, n_classes], or a list of n_outputs \
343             such arrays if n_outputs > 1.
344             The class probabilities of the input samples. The order of the
345             classes corresponds to that in the attribute `classes_`.
346         """
347         check_is_fitted(self)
348         if not all([hasattr(estimator, "predict_proba")
349                     for estimator in self.estimators_]):
350             raise ValueError("The base estimator should implement "
351                              "predict_proba method")
352 
353         results = [estimator.predict_proba(X) for estimator in
354                    self.estimators_]
355         return results
356 
357     def score(self, X, y):
358         """Returns the mean accuracy on the given test data and labels.
359 
360         Parameters
361         ----------
362         X : array-like, shape [n_samples, n_features]
363             Test samples
364 
365         y : array-like, shape [n_samples, n_outputs]
366             True values for X
367 
368         Returns
369         -------
370         scores : float
371             accuracy_score of self.predict(X) versus y
372         """
373         check_is_fitted(self)
374         n_outputs_ = len(self.estimators_)
375         if y.ndim == 1:
376             raise ValueError("y must have at least two dimensions for "
377                              "multi target classification but has only one")
378         if y.shape[1] != n_outputs_:
379             raise ValueError("The number of outputs of Y for fit {0} and"
380                              " score {1} should be same".
381                              format(n_outputs_, y.shape[1]))
382         y_pred = self.predict(X)
383         return np.mean(np.all(y == y_pred, axis=1))
384 
385     def _more_tags(self):
386         # FIXME
387         return {'_skip_test': True}
388 
389 
390 class _BaseChain(BaseEstimator, metaclass=ABCMeta):
391     def __init__(self, base_estimator, order=None, cv=None, random_state=None):
392         self.base_estimator = base_estimator
393         self.order = order
394         self.cv = cv
395         self.random_state = random_state
396 
397     @abstractmethod
398     def fit(self, X, Y):
399         """Fit the model to data matrix X and targets Y.
400 
401         Parameters
402         ----------
403         X : {array-like, sparse matrix}, shape (n_samples, n_features)
404             The input data.
405         Y : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_classes)
406             The target values.
407 
408         Returns
409         -------
410         self : object
411         """
412         X, Y = check_X_y(X, Y, multi_output=True, accept_sparse=True)
413 
414         random_state = check_random_state(self.random_state)
415         check_array(X, accept_sparse=True)
416         self.order_ = self.order
417         if self.order_ is None:
418             self.order_ = np.array(range(Y.shape[1]))
419         elif isinstance(self.order_, str):
420             if self.order_ == 'random':
421                 self.order_ = random_state.permutation(Y.shape[1])
422         elif sorted(self.order_) != list(range(Y.shape[1])):
423                 raise ValueError("invalid order")
424 
425         self.estimators_ = [clone(self.base_estimator)
426                             for _ in range(Y.shape[1])]
427 
428         if self.cv is None:
429             Y_pred_chain = Y[:, self.order_]
430             if sp.issparse(X):
431                 X_aug = sp.hstack((X, Y_pred_chain), format='lil')
432                 X_aug = X_aug.tocsr()
433             else:
434                 X_aug = np.hstack((X, Y_pred_chain))
435 
436         elif sp.issparse(X):
437             Y_pred_chain = sp.lil_matrix((X.shape[0], Y.shape[1]))
438             X_aug = sp.hstack((X, Y_pred_chain), format='lil')
439 
440         else:
441             Y_pred_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], Y.shape[1]))
442             X_aug = np.hstack((X, Y_pred_chain))
443 
444         del Y_pred_chain
445 
446         for chain_idx, estimator in enumerate(self.estimators_):
447             y = Y[:, self.order_[chain_idx]]
448             estimator.fit(X_aug[:, :(X.shape[1] + chain_idx)], y)
449             if self.cv is not None and chain_idx < len(self.estimators_) - 1:
450                 col_idx = X.shape[1] + chain_idx
451                 cv_result = cross_val_predict(
452                     self.base_estimator, X_aug[:, :col_idx],
453                     y=y, cv=self.cv)
454                 if sp.issparse(X_aug):
455                     X_aug[:, col_idx] = np.expand_dims(cv_result, 1)
456                 else:
457                     X_aug[:, col_idx] = cv_result
458 
459         return self
460 
461     def predict(self, X):
462         """Predict on the data matrix X using the ClassifierChain model.
463 
464         Parameters
465         ----------
466         X : {array-like, sparse matrix}, shape (n_samples, n_features)
467             The input data.
468 
469         Returns
470         -------
471         Y_pred : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_classes)
472             The predicted values.
473 
474         """
475         check_is_fitted(self)
476         X = check_array(X, accept_sparse=True)
477         Y_pred_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], len(self.estimators_)))
478         for chain_idx, estimator in enumerate(self.estimators_):
479             previous_predictions = Y_pred_chain[:, :chain_idx]
480             if sp.issparse(X):
481                 if chain_idx == 0:
482                     X_aug = X
483                 else:
484                     X_aug = sp.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
485             else:
486                 X_aug = np.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
487             Y_pred_chain[:, chain_idx] = estimator.predict(X_aug)
488 
489         inv_order = np.empty_like(self.order_)
490         inv_order[self.order_] = np.arange(len(self.order_))
491         Y_pred = Y_pred_chain[:, inv_order]
492 
493         return Y_pred
494 
495 
496 class ClassifierChain(_BaseChain, ClassifierMixin, MetaEstimatorMixin):
497     """A multi-label model that arranges binary classifiers into a chain.
498 
499     Each model makes a prediction in the order specified by the chain using
500     all of the available features provided to the model plus the predictions
501     of models that are earlier in the chain.
502 
503     Read more in the :ref:`User Guide <classifierchain>`.
504 
505     Parameters
506     ----------
507     base_estimator : estimator
508         The base estimator from which the classifier chain is built.
509 
510     order : array-like, shape=[n_outputs] or 'random', optional
511         By default the order will be determined by the order of columns in
512         the label matrix Y.::
513 
514             order = [0, 1, 2, ..., Y.shape[1] - 1]
515 
516         The order of the chain can be explicitly set by providing a list of
517         integers. For example, for a chain of length 5.::
518 
519             order = [1, 3, 2, 4, 0]
520 
521         means that the first model in the chain will make predictions for
522         column 1 in the Y matrix, the second model will make predictions
523         for column 3, etc.
524 
525         If order is 'random' a random ordering will be used.
526 
527     cv : int, cross-validation generator or an iterable, optional \
528     (default=None)
529         Determines whether to use cross validated predictions or true
530         labels for the results of previous estimators in the chain.
531         If cv is None the true labels are used when fitting. Otherwise
532         possible inputs for cv are:
533 
534         - integer, to specify the number of folds in a (Stratified)KFold,
535         - :term:`CV splitter`,
536         - An iterable yielding (train, test) splits as arrays of indices.
537 
538     random_state : int, RandomState instance or None, optional (default=None)
539         If int, random_state is the seed used by the random number generator;
540         If RandomState instance, random_state is the random number generator;
541         If None, the random number generator is the RandomState instance used
542         by `np.random`.
543 
544         The random number generator is used to generate random chain orders.
545 
546     Attributes
547     ----------
548     classes_ : list
549         A list of arrays of length ``len(estimators_)`` containing the
550         class labels for each estimator in the chain.
551 
552     estimators_ : list
553         A list of clones of base_estimator.
554 
555     order_ : list
556         The order of labels in the classifier chain.
557 
558     See also
559     --------
560     RegressorChain: Equivalent for regression
561     MultioutputClassifier: Classifies each output independently rather than
562         chaining.
563 
564     References
565     ----------
566     Jesse Read, Bernhard Pfahringer, Geoff Holmes, Eibe Frank, "Classifier
567     Chains for Multi-label Classification", 2009.
568 
569     """
570 
571     def fit(self, X, Y):
572         """Fit the model to data matrix X and targets Y.
573 
574         Parameters
575         ----------
576         X : {array-like, sparse matrix}, shape (n_samples, n_features)
577             The input data.
578         Y : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_classes)
579             The target values.
580 
581         Returns
582         -------
583         self : object
584         """
585         super().fit(X, Y)
586         self.classes_ = [estimator.classes_
587                          for chain_idx, estimator
588                          in enumerate(self.estimators_)]
589         return self
590 
591     @if_delegate_has_method('base_estimator')
592     def predict_proba(self, X):
593         """Predict probability estimates.
594 
595         Parameters
596         ----------
597         X : {array-like, sparse matrix}, shape (n_samples, n_features)
598 
599         Returns
600         -------
601         Y_prob : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_classes)
602         """
603         X = check_array(X, accept_sparse=True)
604         Y_prob_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], len(self.estimators_)))
605         Y_pred_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], len(self.estimators_)))
606         for chain_idx, estimator in enumerate(self.estimators_):
607             previous_predictions = Y_pred_chain[:, :chain_idx]
608             if sp.issparse(X):
609                 X_aug = sp.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
610             else:
611                 X_aug = np.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
612             Y_prob_chain[:, chain_idx] = estimator.predict_proba(X_aug)[:, 1]
613             Y_pred_chain[:, chain_idx] = estimator.predict(X_aug)
614         inv_order = np.empty_like(self.order_)
615         inv_order[self.order_] = np.arange(len(self.order_))
616         Y_prob = Y_prob_chain[:, inv_order]
617 
618         return Y_prob
619 
620     @if_delegate_has_method('base_estimator')
621     def decision_function(self, X):
622         """Evaluate the decision_function of the models in the chain.
623 
624         Parameters
625         ----------
626         X : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_features)
627 
628         Returns
629         -------
630         Y_decision : array-like, shape (n_samples, n_classes )
631             Returns the decision function of the sample for each model
632             in the chain.
633         """
634         Y_decision_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], len(self.estimators_)))
635         Y_pred_chain = np.zeros((X.shape[0], len(self.estimators_)))
636         for chain_idx, estimator in enumerate(self.estimators_):
637             previous_predictions = Y_pred_chain[:, :chain_idx]
638             if sp.issparse(X):
639                 X_aug = sp.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
640             else:
641                 X_aug = np.hstack((X, previous_predictions))
642             Y_decision_chain[:, chain_idx] = estimator.decision_function(X_aug)
643             Y_pred_chain[:, chain_idx] = estimator.predict(X_aug)
644 
645         inv_order = np.empty_like(self.order_)
646         inv_order[self.order_] = np.arange(len(self.order_))
647         Y_decision = Y_decision_chain[:, inv_order]
648 
649         return Y_decision
650 
651     def _more_tags(self):
652         return {'_skip_test': True,
653                 'multioutput_only': True}
654

Old-style hookimpl warning has no location information
The old-style hookimpl deprecation warning from #9118 has no way to see where the culprit is implemented. I'm now getting:

```
pytest.PytestDeprecationWarning: The hookimpl pytest_configure uses old-style configuration options (marks or attributes).
Please use the pytest.hookimpl(trylast=True) decorator instead
```

with no easy way to figure out what the problem is. I have 12 plugins installed, all of which might have a `pytest_configure`, and I'd rather not have to find out manually which one is the culprit. The error message should show either the plugin that's coming from, or at least the Python file it's in.

86 class ExitCode(enum.IntEnum):

108 class ConftestImportFailure(Exception):
109     def __init__(
110         self,
111         path: Path,
112         excinfo: Tuple[Type[Exception], Exception, TracebackType],
113     ) -> None:
114         super().__init__(path, excinfo)
115         self.path = path
116         self.excinfo = excinfo
117 
118     def __str__(self) -> str:
119         return "{}: {} (from {})".format(
120             self.excinfo[0].__name__, self.excinfo[1], self.path
121         )
122 
123 
124 def filter_traceback_for_conftest_import_failure(
125     entry: _pytest._code.TracebackEntry,
126 ) -> bool:
127     """Filter tracebacks entries which point to pytest internals or importlib.
128 
129     Make a special case for importlib because we use it to import test modules and conftest files
130     in _pytest.pathlib.import_path.
131     """
132     return filter_traceback(entry) and "importlib" not in str(entry.path).split(os.sep)
133 
134 
135 def main(
136     args: Optional[Union[List[str], "os.PathLike[str]"]] = None,
137     plugins: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, _PluggyPlugin]]] = None,
138 ) -> Union[int, ExitCode]:
139     """Perform an in-process test run.
140 
141     :param args: List of command line arguments.
142     :param plugins: List of plugin objects to be auto-registered during initialization.
143 
144     :returns: An exit code.
145     """
146     try:
147         try:
148             config = _prepareconfig(args, plugins)
149         except ConftestImportFailure as e:
150             exc_info = ExceptionInfo.from_exc_info(e.excinfo)
151             tw = TerminalWriter(sys.stderr)
152             tw.line(f"ImportError while loading conftest '{e.path}'.", red=True)
153             exc_info.traceback = exc_info.traceback.filter(
154                 filter_traceback_for_conftest_import_failure
155             )
156             exc_repr = (
157                 exc_info.getrepr(style="short", chain=False)
158                 if exc_info.traceback
159                 else exc_info.exconly()
160             )
161             formatted_tb = str(exc_repr)
162             for line in formatted_tb.splitlines():
163                 tw.line(line.rstrip(), red=True)
164             return ExitCode.USAGE_ERROR
165         else:
166             try:
167                 ret: Union[ExitCode, int] = config.hook.pytest_cmdline_main(
168                     config=config
169                 )
170                 try:
171                     return ExitCode(ret)
172                 except ValueError:
173                     return ret
174             finally:
175                 config._ensure_unconfigure()
176     except UsageError as e:
177         tw = TerminalWriter(sys.stderr)
178         for msg in e.args:
179             tw.line(f"ERROR: {msg}\n", red=True)
180         return ExitCode.USAGE_ERROR
181 
182 
183 def console_main() -> int:
184     """The CLI entry point of pytest.
185 
186     This function is not meant for programmable use; use `main()` instead.
187     """
188     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/signal.html#note-on-sigpipe
189     try:
190         code = main()
191         sys.stdout.flush()
192         return code
193     except BrokenPipeError:
194         # Python flushes standard streams on exit; redirect remaining output
195         # to devnull to avoid another BrokenPipeError at shutdown
196         devnull = os.open(os.devnull, os.O_WRONLY)
197         os.dup2(devnull, sys.stdout.fileno())
198         return 1  # Python exits with error code 1 on EPIPE
199 
200 
201 class cmdline:  # compatibility namespace
202     main = staticmethod(main)
203

205 def filename_arg(path: str, optname: str) -> str:
206     """Argparse type validator for filename arguments.
207 
208     :path: Path of filename.
209     :optname: Name of the option.
210     """
211     if os.path.isdir(path):
212         raise UsageError(f"{optname} must be a filename, given: {path}")
213     return path
214 
215 
216 def directory_arg(path: str, optname: str) -> str:
217     """Argparse type validator for directory arguments.
218 
219     :path: Path of directory.
220     :optname: Name of the option.
221     """
222     if not os.path.isdir(path):
223         raise UsageError(f"{optname} must be a directory, given: {path}")
224     return path

270 def get_config(
271     args: Optional[List[str]] = None,
272     plugins: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, _PluggyPlugin]]] = None,
273 ) -> "Config":
274     # subsequent calls to main will create a fresh instance
275     pluginmanager = PytestPluginManager()
276     config = Config(
277         pluginmanager,
278         invocation_params=Config.InvocationParams(
279             args=args or (),
280             plugins=plugins,
281             dir=Path.cwd(),
282         ),
283     )
284 
285     if args is not None:
286         # Handle any "-p no:plugin" args.
287         pluginmanager.consider_preparse(args, exclude_only=True)
288 
289     for spec in default_plugins:
290         pluginmanager.import_plugin(spec)
291 
292     return config
293

295 def get_plugin_manager() -> "PytestPluginManager":
296     """Obtain a new instance of the
297     :py:class:`pytest.PytestPluginManager`, with default plugins
298     already loaded.
299 
300     This function can be used by integration with other tools, like hooking
301     into pytest to run tests into an IDE.
302     """
303     return get_config().pluginmanager
304 
305 
306 def _prepareconfig(
307     args: Optional[Union[List[str], "os.PathLike[str]"]] = None,
308     plugins: Optional[Sequence[Union[str, _PluggyPlugin]]] = None,
309 ) -> "Config":
310     if args is None:
311         args = sys.argv[1:]
312     elif isinstance(args, os.PathLike):
313         args = [os.fspath(args)]
314     elif not isinstance(args, list):
315         msg = (  # type:ignore[unreachable]
316             "`args` parameter expected to be a list of strings, got: {!r} (type: {})"
317         )
318         raise TypeError(msg.format(args, type(args)))
319 
320     config = get_config(args, plugins)
321     pluginmanager = config.pluginmanager
322     try:
323         if plugins:
324             for plugin in plugins:
325                 if isinstance(plugin, str):
326                     pluginmanager.consider_pluginarg(plugin)
327                 else:
328                     pluginmanager.register(plugin)
329         config = pluginmanager.hook.pytest_cmdline_parse(
330             pluginmanager=pluginmanager, args=args
331         )
332         return config
333     except BaseException:
334         config._ensure_unconfigure()
335         raise
336
